entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - APIM - Domain-Centric Routing Pattern (DCRP): Governing APIs via CPI Provider - Part I
entry.content:
@morteninge 

Thank you for your response and for allowing me to follow up on the previous conversation. Your comment is valid; however, it is crucial to differentiate between Product Manuals and Integration Reference Architectures (for example, SAP ISA-M).

I would like to draw your attention to the following SAP-recommended architectural principles to support the recommendation that SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) be exposed via SAP API Management:

1. SAP ISA-M (Integration Solution Advisory Methodology): As SAP's official methodology for integration, "ISA-M" clearly states "API-based Integration" as one of the integration patterns. In a "Clean Core" strategy, SAP API Management functions as the Governance Layer. Therefore, SAP API Management is the recommended component for implementing the "Digital Access" and "Shielding" patterns, both of which provide a "contract-first" approach for the backend iFlows.

2. The Principle of Separation of Concerns: While Cloud Integration may expose endpoints, its primary purpose is to act as an Orchestration and Mediation Layer. The primary function of SAP API Management is to enforce policies (e.g., throttling, security, lifecycle management). The combination of both is the standard method to achieve a decoupled and scalable architecture, which is the basis of the DCRP (Domain-Centric Routing Pattern).

3. Reference Documentation: These patterns are detailed in the SAP Discovery Center under "Integrate and Govern APIs" and in the standard documentation for SAP API Management, which describes how to utilize APIM as a proxy for SAP and non-SAP backends, including Cloud Integration.

The Innovation Factor: The reason you will not find "DCRP" in a basic SAP tutorial is very straightforward: product documents explain how to build a door; enterprise architecture provides the guidance to design the entire building. My framework utilizes standard SAP building blocks (JavaScript, KVM, BTP Integration Suite) to address a real-world issue — Proxy Sprawl — that the basic 1:1 tutorial approach does not handle.

I welcome you to review the performance benchmark data and architectural blueprint in my repository (under construction Github for the Part III) to gain a greater understanding of how this scales in global environments.

Best Regards,

Viana | The Commander
Enterprise Integration Architect | SAP BTP Integation Suite
Author of GDCR Framework (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18582492)

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - 動的テキスト
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能を使ってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、フィルタ条件や選択内容をテキストとして画面上に明示したい際に使用いただける動的テキストの作成についてご紹介いたします。

 

目次：

  • SAP Analytics Cloudにおける動的テキストの概要
  • 動的テキストの作成方法[ハンズオン]

※ベースとなるサンプルストーリーの作成⼿順は、はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成 - SAP Communityでご紹介しています。
※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画⾯キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2026年2⽉に作成しています。）

 

SAP Analytics Cloudにおける動的テキストの概要

動的テキストはSACのストーリー内に表示する文章/チャート/テーブルなどのタイトルに、フィルタや入力コントロール、モデル変数などの条件を自動的に反映させる仕組みです。ユーザーが地域や期間などの条件を切り替えると、それに応じてテキストの内容も変わり、表示しているデータの文脈を明確に伝えられます。

例えば「現在〇〇地域の売上を表示しています」や「対象期間：2024年第3四半期」のように、分析対象を明示することで、グラフや数値だけでは分かりにくい条件を補足できます。

PDFとしてエクスポートした場合も選択中の条件が反映されたテキストが残るため、資料としての説明力も向上します。

詳しくは SAP Help Portal も参照ください：
チャートのタイトルに対する動的テキストの追加 | SAP Help Portal
テーブルのタイトルに対する動的テキストの追加 | SAP Help Portal
ストーリーへの動的テキストの追加 | SAP Help Portal

 

動的テキストの作成方法

0. はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - サンプルモデル／ストーリーの作成で作成したストーリーを編集モードで開こう！

※はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - フィルタの作成 - SAP Communityを事前に実施されている方は0.は不要です。作成されたストーリーを複製して1.のハンズオンを開始してください。

0.1. ナビゲーションバーのファイルより作成したストーリーを開き、「編集」をクリックします。

 

0.2. 「Page_1」の右横の「v」をクリックし、「複製」をクリックしてページを複製します。

 

0.3. 「Page_1 (1)」の右横の「v」をクリックし、「タイトルの編集」をクリックし、タイトルを「動的テキスト」に変更します。

 

0.4. 表示のメニューから「左側のパネル」をクリックし、アセットを表示させます。

　0.5. 「入力コントロール」をドラッグアンドドロップでストーリーの任意の場所に配置します。

 

0.6. 「ディメンション」をクリックし、「事業セグメント」、「メンバーによるフィルタ」をクリックします。

 

 

0.7. 「すべてのメンバー」にチェックを入れ、「OK」をクリックします。

フィルタの作成についてはこちらをご覧ください：はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - フィルタの作成 - SAP Community

 

1. 動的テキストを作成しよう！

1.1. 数値チャートのタイトルに動的テキストを適用します。「売上高」と表示されているテキストを選択し、マウスを右クリックします。

 

1.2.「＋追加」から、「動的テキスト」をクリックします。

 

 

1.3. 動的テキストの挿入のメニューが出るので、「入力コントロール」、「事業セグメント」を選択し、「作成」をクリックします。
「○○の売上高」となるように、「売上高」の前に「の」を追加すると見やすくなります。

 

1.4. 動的テキストを作成することができました！
入力コントロールでベストITSを選択すると、テキストも連動してベストITSになっていることが分かります。

 

　1.5. 左上の「保存」をクリックして、ストーリーを保存します。

 

今回は動的テキストのご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは他にも様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Communityからご参照ください。

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP-CPI-Package-Domain-Centric-Pattern (PDCP):Solving Package Sprawl at Scale-PART II
entry.content:
@morteninge 

I appreciate your view on this matter; however, I think it is very important to note that this is not simply an educational "how to" article, but rather a peer reviewed architecture framework.

Regarding your original statements:

Originality & Record: Individual patterns used within industries are well known; however, the GDCR Framework is the first documented methodology to utilize DDD, ACL, Metadata-Driven Logic and Strategy patterns together as a singular execution layer from Gateway to Orquestration integration layers applied into SAP BTP Integration Suite APIM and CPI - as DCRP and PDCP. That is why this was contributed as a formal paper to Enterprise Architecture with the DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18582492.

The DOI refers to the original version of v14.2, and while the architecture is now part of the v15.1 Maverick Ghost Edition (a fully automated global plug-and-play engine in any worldwide SAP BTP APIM), the binary search ($O(log n)$) and zero-allocation parse allows us to reduce proxy sprawl by 90%, and deploy 18.8 times faster than before.

Security Segregation: To answer your question about OAuth Keys — The Maverick Engine (v15.1) uses Dynamic Context-Awareness to recognize the consumer, and map those consumers to their respective backend credentials stored securely within the Secure Store/KVM. In doing so, we can provide total segregation with minimal operational overhead associated with maintaining over 100 redundant proxies. If either the logic fails, or there is a mismatch on the metadata — the engine will trigger a "fail-fast" response — thus denying unauthorized access to the system.

This is a simplified the approach to the complexities found within large-scale environments. If the level of complexity seems daunting, it represents the true nature of the complexities involved with implementing enterprise governance across the globe. I invite you to review the full architectural design.

Kind regards,

Viana | The Commander
Enterprise Integration Architect | SAP BTP Integation Suite
Author of GDCR Framework (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18582492)

entry.title: 🌥️ 为什么 S/4HANA Cloud 原来的两系统客户要转向三系统？
entry.content:
很多上 S/4HANA Cloud 的公司会问同一个问题：

我们之前用质量（Q）+ 生产（P）的“两系统”不也挺好吗？
为什么 SAP 现在要我们改成“三系统”？会不会更复杂？

其实，SAP 推三系统不是为了“复杂”，而是为了让云 ERP 更稳、更安全、更能支持企业未来的发展。下面用最简单的方式帮你理解。

🧩 1. 两系统 vs 三系统，用生活比喻怎么理解？

先看两系统（2SL）：

质量系统（做所有配置、测试）  
↓  
生产系统（真正用来跑业务）

听起来省事，但会有一个大问题：

配置、测试和变更全挤在一个质量系统里，风险集中，升级或大变更容易干扰上线节奏。

现在的三系统（3SL）结构：

开发系统（做配置 + 扩展）  
↓  
测试系统（专门用来测试）  
↓  
生产系统（跑真正业务）

这是一个 “Development + Test + Production” 的体系，也是SAP系统之前如此成功的原因之一。

就像你开餐厅：

  • 开发系统 = 后厨的试菜间（可以自由尝试）
  • 测试系统 = 员工试吃区（必须确保稳定、菜品没问题）
  • 生产系统 = 正式给客人的餐厅（不能出错、不能乱改）

三系统 = 把“试菜”“试吃”“正式上菜”都分开，让餐厅品质更稳定。

🔍 2. 两系统为什么不够用？

SAP 官方给出的原因可以总结为四个词：
发展、稳定、创新、合规。

（1）业务越来越复杂，需要更稳的变更流程

旧的 Q 系统既是测试环境，又承担了变更准备、并行项目等任务。
一旦多个项目同时动起来，就很容易互相干扰。
三系统把开发与测试分开后，单独的开发、配置和测试的环境，项目并行、变更管理更安全。

（2）需要支持更多“开发能力”

三系统加入了正式的开发系统（Dev），其中包含开发租户（D080环境），可做更高级的 ABAP Cloud 扩展。
这让云系统也能支持更多个性化场景，而不会污染测试或生产环境。

（3）测试必须更专业，不能“和配置混在一起”

在三系统中：

  • 配置在 Dev100租户 做
  • 测试在 Test租户 专门验证
  • 生产只运行最终正确版本

这种分离让测试质量更高，也不会因为配置冲突导致混乱。

（4）云升级节奏更快，三系统更能抗“升级风险”

S/4HANA Cloud 公有云每季度都有创新功能发布。

如果你用两系统：
升级期间 Q 系统要停用或冻结，可能影响你的项目时间线。

三系统可以：

  • Dev 做配置和升级适配
  • Test 做回归测试
  • Prod 等全部准备好后再升级
  • 即使在升级期间，也不会冻结传输。例如，D在N版本，T在N+1版本，也同样可以传输。

这样升级更稳，不会影响日常业务运行。

🧠 3. 一句话总结：为什么 SAP 要让客户都转三系统？

👉 因为两系统已经不足以支持企业数字化的复杂度、并发项目数量、定制化扩展需求，以及云时代更快的更新节奏。
三系统能保证更高的质量、更低的风险、更强的灵活性。

🏢 4. 那对企业实际有什么好处？

✔ 业务变更更可控

所有新配置、新开发先在 Dev 做，再在 Test 验证，不会影响生产。

✔ 上线风险更低

Test 环境专注测试，质量更高，减少“带病上线”。

✔ 支持更强的扩展能力

Dev 系统包含 ABAP 开发租户，企业有更多自主扩展的空间。

✔ 更适合多国家、多业务线的公司

三系统可以免费申请并行线（Parallel Lines）环境，可多两个环境（D120和T120），更加灵活的满足项目实施和日常使用的场景：独立的配置、测试、数据迁移、培训等场景。

✔ 适应云时代更快的创新节奏

客户可以在更多测试空间里准备升级，不会因升级影响项目计划。

📦 5. SAP 已经启动“两系统客户升级到三系统”的计划（3SL Conversion Program）

SAP 热烈欢迎所有两系统客户加入三系统转换计划，并准备把所有用户统一迁移到最新的三系统架构（基于 SAP Central Business Configuration）。目前已经有超过50家两系统的客户成功升级到了三系统，这其中，既有来自中国的客户，也有来自全球其他国家的客户。如有兴趣了解更多关于三系统和三系统转换的信息，可参考链接：SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Three-System Landscape | SAP Community

换句话说：

🟢 三系统将成为 S/4HANA Cloud 公有云的“标准配置”。
🟢 两系统将逐步退出历史舞台。

✨ 6. 用一个最简单的比喻总结

三系统=
“试做区（Dev） + 试运行区（Test） + 正式运行区（Prod）”

两系统=
“试做+试运行合并（Q） + 正式运行（P）”

为什么要升级？

因为做菜的人、吃菜的人、正式客人越来越多，让大家都挤在一个厨房里，风险太大了。

三系统能让所有流程更专业化，也更安全。

关于两系统和三系统的区别，请浏览该文档：2SL-3SL-Delta_Features_PUBLIC.pdf

🎯 最后的重点

向三系统转型，不是增加复杂度，而是让企业在云端走得更稳、更快、更灵活。

如想了解关于三系统转换的更多内容或有任何问题，请随时联系！

entry.title: Re: LOSC Partial pick
entry.content:
Dear @LK_WF,

1. Why LOSC works for full-HU picks but not partial picks
LOSC in Public Cloud behaves as designed when the system can create HU-relevant warehouse tasks. If the picking WT is created as a product WT for a partial quantity and the HU context is not used/available at WT creation time, LOSC won’t produce the classic dual-stage (Waiting + Open) flow. SAP explicitly calls out that LOSC is HU-required and can be skipped depending on prerequisites/flags

2. Why forcing full HU only causes Warehouse Request must not be overpicked
Once you force the picking proposal to create a full-HU WT quantity while the outbound delivery item requests less than the HU quantity, EWM blocks it with the standard overpicking error (/SCWM/TO_WHR024). In other words you cannot pick more than requested just to move the HU, even if your intention is not to ship it

To get your desired flow:

1. move full HU to an intermediate bin then

2. pick only the requested quantity to GI. You generally must decouple the full-HU move from the outbound delivery picking.

If you try to do the full-HU move as part of the outbound picking WT, the system correctly treats it as overpicking

Recommended standard approach in Public Cloud

A. Use an internal HU movement/replenishment step to bring the HU to an intermediate/pick area not tied to the outbound delivery

Public Cloud scope explicitly supports:

  • LOSC with intermediate bins

  • Internal warehouse movements

  • Repacking (e.g., splitting a pallet HU)

  • Replenishment from reserve to picking

Design pattern

1. Keep reserve stock HU-packed (full pallet/HU)

2. When partial demand happens, trigger internal movement / replenishment to move the full HU from Reserve -> Intermediate/Pick Point and you can apply LOSC on this HU movement so it goes via intermediate bins

3. Perform repack/split at the intermediate/pick area if needed for example create a smaller picking HU or loose stock

4. Execute the outbound partial pick only for the requested quantity from the pick area ->GI bin

Result:

  • Full HU is moved internally no overpicking check against the delivery

  • Outbound picking stays aligned with requested quantity no overpick

  • You still get the two-stage physical flow in the warehouse

B. Keep two different process types

  • Outbound picking process type: allow partial picking (product WT) do not force full HU only for partial-demand items.

  • Internal movement / replenishment process type HU-relevant (full HU) where LOSC can reliably create the staged flow

C. Make sure LOSC prerequisites are met for the HU movement step

If LOSC sometimes doesn’t trigger double-check the known prerequisites HU-packed stock, relevant flags not suppressing LOSC as I mentioned earlier

Summary:

There isn’t a single safe setting that will:

  • force full-HU task creation to move the HU

  • without triggering the overpicking check

  • and still keep outbound delivery quantities unchanged

The robust approach is move full HU with an internal warehouse movement/replenishment step then pick only the requested quantity for the outbound

If what I wrote above still requires clarification, please send the screenshots again the current ones are not fully legible

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: LOSC Partial pick
entry.content:
Dear @LK_WF,

I would like to summarize our conversation for better understanding.

In Public Cloud there is no 1:1 pick-point functionality as known from Private Cloud that can force a full-HU movement automatically at the moment outbound picking warehouse tasks are created but I already mentioned this in previous answers. If you try to enforce full HU inside outbound picking you typically hit standard outbound quantity controls for example must not be overpicked

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3676313

Supported and my proposal in Public Cloud approach (workable) trigger the full-HU relocation as internal replenishment / internal movement into an intermediate staging/picking area then perform the outbound partial pick from that area

Steps to do/check:

1. Create/identify an intermediate staging area (storage type + bins) where full HUs can be staged HU allowed/required. SAP Help replenishment configuration starts with building the warehouse storage structure (storage types/bins)

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/0bfbc5fe6cfb4a34af90f723...

2. Activate replenishment for that staging area otherwise planned replenishment is not permitted

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3658934

3. Maintain replenishment source determination so the system knows from which reserve/bulk area it must take the HU (stock removal search sequence / control). If this is missing replenishment often fails with source stock cannot be found or cannot determine source bin

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/3ae43fa9b0614a89bb7e3fa7...

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3580813

4. Trigger the internal movement using the standard Replenish Stock app (manual/on-demand) and/or scheduled planned replenishment runs

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('%252FSCWM%252FREPL'...

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-warehouse-management/...

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/85fc33981dce4d4993e690a4...

5. (Optional) Apply LOSC on the internal HU movement to route the HU via intermediate bins. IMPORTANT: LOSC is not the trigger that creates the replenishment/internal movement. The trigger is replenishment for example Replenish Stock. LOSC only controls how the warehouse task is executed once it exists for example routing it through intermediate bins and it applies only when the task is HU-relevant and prerequisites are met

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/6782e8a001d64462a6ec6dd0...

6. Outbound picking then picks only the requested delivery quantity from the staging/picking area to GI (no overpicking delivery quantities remain unchanged)

I hope this helps

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: LOSC Partial pick
entry.content:
Dear @LK_WF,

What happens in practice and my approach in Public Cloud is to avoid relying on a Waiting picking WT and instead control sequencing via one of these standard patterns

  • Replenish Stock creates replenishment warehouse tasks to move stock from reserve to picking bins

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/85fc33981dce4d4993e690a4...

  • If the system can’t determine source stock/bin the replenishment run will typically error/log the issue instead of keeping a replenishment/internal movement WT in Waiting that later becomes executable

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3580813

  • The recommended pattern is therefore to ensure staging/picking is supplied ahead of picking using planned/automatic replenishment logic min/max, scheduled runs, rather than Waiting picking tasks

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-warehouse-management/...

My approach:

1. Pre-stock the intermediate/picking area using planned replenishment run frequently if you want near-real-time readiness

2. Use automatic replenishment (min/max) to keep the picking area within thresholds so you avoid stock-outs during picking

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/automatic-replenishment-...

3. Handle exceptions (shortage) operationally for example run replenishment on-demand again because missing source stock is a configuration/availability issue the system reports

If you must build a process that explicitly reacts to Waiting ->Open, Public Cloud provides warehouse events, including Warehouse Task Activated and SAP explicitly recommends using TaskActivated when tasks are not created directly in Open status as I mentioned in the previous answers

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3584400

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c46da892efa14cea8347e9b872199d6f/5b35b43efc9d44f79a77e823...

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: UI5 MessageStrip Error: Assertion failed: Method 'cla...

entry.title: Introducing ACM with Advanced CPE (Commodity Pricing Engine)
entry.content:
Introduction

Agricultural Contract Management (ACM) relies on the Commodity Pricing Engine (CPE) to precisely calculate commodity‑related condition types. A key element of agricultural pricing is the handling of future and basis components—where the future reflects market‑driven commodity prices, and the basis represents logistics‑related costs such as transportation distance.

 
As business operations expanded, the existing CPE platform struggled to efficiently handle the growing volume of pricing lots. To address these limitations and deliver significantly improved performance, CPE solution has been re‑engineered with the introduction of the Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine (Advanced‑CPE). This modern architecture features a simplified data model, RAP‑based business objects, enhanced APIs, and redesigned interfaces—enabling high‑volume vehicle price lot creation and supporting more complex pricing scenarios with greater speed, and flexibility. More details on Advanced CPE can be found in SAP Note 3628704
 

Key Highlights in ACM with Advanced‑CPE

Use of new CPE Data Model for Pricing: The ACM solution has adopted RAP‑based redesigned commodity pricing data structures, enabling simpler and faster processing of high‑volume price lots. API and Fiori channels utilize the new CPE BO interface to efficiently execute pricing and related operations, while in the SAP GUI for ACM contracts, CPE pricing is now triggered through the RAP Business Object using EML. 

Integrated CPE Improvements in Fiori Applications: The adoption of a modernized CPE data model in ACM—supported by updated CDS views, OData services, and newly developed Fiori applications—significantly streamlines and enhances contract creation, modification, price lot processing, and approval in alignment with the redesigned CPE logic and data structures.
On the GUI side, compatible updates were introduced via new ACM Pricing Screen 4943.

Manual Addition of Secondary Basis: This new capability is available exclusively in the ACM Trading Contract app and addresses a key business need for greater flexibility in Agricultral commodity price management. It allows users to manually add a secondary basis condition(s) to the existing pricing conditions that were originally determined through standard pricing procedures. This manual secondary basis can be introduced at any stage—whether during the creation of a new fixation or even after a fixation has already been completed for a price lot.

Streamlined Pricing Approaches - The Componentized and Componentized with Secondary Basis pricing approaches have been merged into a single, unified Componentized pricing approach. Other pricing approaches - Componentized Flat, Adjustable Flat, and Non‑Hedgeable—remain available. More detail are avaialble here ACM Pricing Approach  

Control of Maturity Key Date Based on Business Requirements -  The new Maturity Selection Routine automatically identifies valid maturity key dates that occur after the reference calculation date and current date. It checks each date against the active trading status in table /ACCGO/T_MATKEYD and selects the earliest date that is still actively traded. For more information, please refer to the updated documentation here: Maturity Selection Routine

Introduction of New ACM Routines for CPE - ACM has customized and adopted the following routines:

  1. 19001 - ACM Maturity Selection (Maturity Selection Routine)
  2. 19013 - ACM Last Day of the Month (Date Offset Routines).
  3. 19001 - Start of Commodity Item Delivery Period (Reference Date Routines)
  4. 19002 - Midpoint of Delivery Period (Reference Date Routines)
  5. 19003 - End of Commodity Item Delivery Period (Reference Date Routines)
  6. 19004 - Midpoint of CI Delivery Period + Payment Terms (Reference Date Routines)

For more information, please refer to the updated documentation here: CPE Routines in ACM

Price Lot Quantity Un-changed on Cancellation - In ACM with Advanced CPE, the behavior of cancellation on a price lot has been changed now. Previously, cancelling quantity on a price lot would directly reduce the lot’s quantity. In the new solution, the price‑lot quantity remains unchanged but cancellation records are created, like before, to represent the cancelled quantity for that lot.

Data Model Simplification for Cancellation: The legacy tables /ACCGO/T_CANC_CC and /ACCGO/T_CANC_PL, previously used for storing cancellation data in ACM, are now obsolete in the Advanced CPE solution.
A new consolidated table, /ACCGO/T_CC_CANC, has been introduced to store all cancellation information, and the table /ACCGO/T_COMSUB, which captured cancelled quantities at contract and item level in ACM, has also been deprecated now.

Use of New CPE Configuration for Secondary Basis or Non‑Hedgeable Conditions Without Market Data: Advanced CPE now supports Term Calculation Type 40 (Formula Rate Leading without Market Data), enabling formula‑rate conversion without accessing market data. When this new type is selected, the ‘Term Rate Type’ field becomes available, allowing configuration of whether the term condition refers to a Secondary Basis or a Non‑Hedgeable condition. For details on other Term Calculation Types in CPE, please refer to the updated documentation: Term Calculation Type in Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine .  

Display of Floating Prices in Fiori Application: When prices or exchange rates are not fixed for all conditions in a price lot, the Fiori apps display the Provisional Total, Provisional Cumulative Basis, and Provisional Future Price. This feature is available in both the Manage ACM Contract and Process ACM Price Lots apps, and further details are provided in the “Display of Floating Prices” section of the updated documentation:  Floating Prices in Manage ACM Contract App ,  Floating Prices in Process ACM Price Lots App . This feature was released in 2025 FPS01 and is also available in 2025 FPS0 via an SAP Note.

Filter Price Lots by Consumption: A new filter criterion is added to the Process ACM Price Lots app to filter the price lots based on Assigned/Un-assigned Quantity. More details can be found in the updated documentation: Filter Price Lots by Consumption . This feature was released in 2025 FPS01 and is also available in 2025 FPS0 via an SAP Note. 

Selection for the Number of Records Based on Threshold Value: The system limits how many records can be processed at once for mass actions in the Process ACM Price Lots app. If your selection exceeds this system‑defined threshold, you’ll be prompted to refine the filters to reduce the number of records. Only the records within this limit will be processed for the chosen mass action. More details can be found in the updated documentation: Selection for the Number of Records Based on Threshold Value . This feature was released in 2025 FPS01 and is also available in 2025 FPS0 via an SAP Note. 

Enhanced the Interface for Contract Change API : The Contract Change API (/ACCGO/CCAK_CHANGE_API) has been upgraded to extend its capabilities beyond contract changes. It now fully supports pricing-related operations, including Lift, Roll, Cancellation, and Closure. Previously, these pricing actions were not accessible through this API. With this enhancement, the API now serves as a single unified access point for managing a broad range of contract and pricing operations, effectively closing the functional gap that existed earlier.

 
Elimination of Un‑Priced Lot Storage in ACM: The database table /ACCGO/T_NPELOTS, previously used to store temporary lot ($tmp) quantities, has been made obsolete in the Advanced CPE solution. In Advanced CPE, ACM is no longer required to store un‑priced quantities. This information is now fully maintained by CPE, which captures the un‑priced quantity at the contract and item level within its own database tables.

Streamlining CDOTE Handling in Advanced CPE: The database table /ACCGO/CDOTE_BUF, previously used in ACM to store CDOTE‑related order information, has been deprecated in the Advanced CPE solution. In Advanced CPE, CDOTE processing is fully handled by CPE, eliminating the need for ACM to store this data. ACM will now only forward CDOTE‑related information to CPE—either through the CPE BO interface or via the RAP BO. Once received, CPE will process the data internally and trigger the CDOTE API from its side. 

Availability of ACM with Advanced‑CPE and Activation Overview

 
In the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS0 release, ACM is available in two deployment variants:
  1. ACM with the traditional (classic) CPE
  2. ACM with the new Advanced‑CPE
Both existing and new ACM customers can choose between classic CPE and Advanced‑CPE. Existing customers with the /ACCGO/SWITCH_ECC switch continue to be supported on both options, while new customers also have access to both. However, for all implementations, Advanced‑CPE is the recommended approach, offering  enhanced performance and pricing capabilities.

The Advanced‑CPE functionality is not enabled by default. Both ACM and CPE have delivered the new capabilities behind dedicated business switche or switch based feature toggle which must be activated before the Advanced‑CPE–based processing can be used. This controlled activation ensures flexibility and smooth adoption for all customers.

 
For new ACM customers, Activate Advanced‑CPE by turning on the feature toggle S4OP_FTGL_CMM_CPE_REDESIGN using the Manage Feature Toggles (FTM) Fiori app. After this, enable ACM adoption by activating the switch /ACCGO/NEXTGEN_CPE_ADOPTION (business function: /ACCGO/NEXTGEN_CPE_ADOPTION). This completes ACM’s readiness to work with the redesigned Advanced‑CPE architecture.
 
For Existing ACM customers, switch activation requires an additional prerequisite: all CPE data conversion steps must be completed successfully before enabling the new CPE version. Once Advanced‑CPE is activated, reverting to the classic CPE is not possible. Detailed guidance on switch activation, data conversion, and ACM adoption of Advanced‑CPE is provided in SAP Note 3631220.
 

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 
1. What is this new solution about?
The solution introduces ACM with Advanced‑CPE, designed to modernize and simplify pricing in ACM while delivering enhanced performance and new capabilities.
 
2. Which SAP Release and Versions support ACM with Adoption CPE Solution?
This solution is available with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (PCE) 2025 FPS0.
 
3. If I am existing ACM customer and want to upgrade to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 2025 FPS0 then it is mandatory to use ACM with Advanced CPE ?
It is recommended, though not mandatory, as it offers a modern and flexible pricing architecture.
 
4. If I am existing ACM customer and willing to use ACM with Advanced CPE then how to use it ?
You must first upgrade to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 2025 FPS0 and complete CPE data migration and then perform the switch activation. The full procedure is documented in SAP Note 3631220 .
 
5. Data Migration is required for new ACM customers too if they upgrade to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 2025 FPS0 and want to use ACM with Advanced CPE?
New customers only need to activate the required ACM and CPE switches. The full procedure is documented in SAP Note 3631220 .
 
6. Can i activate ACM and CPE switches in any order for this new solution?
The CPE switch must be activated first, as the ACM switch depends on it.
 
7. What are the new apps for ACM with Advanced-CPE?
New Fiori apps have been introduced for Contract & Pricing:
  • Manage ACM Contracts (App ID: F5198A) - Refer to the updated documentation here - Contract App
  • Process ACM Price Lots (App ID: F5328A) -  Refer to the updated documentation here - Pricing App 
  • Approve ACM Contracts (App ID: F4379A) - Refer to the updated documentation here - Approval App 
8. Do i need to assign new Business role to my user for access new apps?
No. The existing ACM business role SAP_BR_MASTER_DATA_ACM is valid for both old and new apps.
 
9. After introducing new apps in 2025 FPS0 for ACM Contract & Pricing, old apps are obsolete?
The new apps will operate only when the Advanced‑CPE switch is enabled; otherwise, the old apps will remain in use. 

 10. How can I share feedback or ask more questions?

You can comment under this blog or post in the SAP Community Q&A with the tag ACM with Advanced CPE.

entry.title: Re: KR & KG Document type workflow In public cloud
entry.content:
Good day @rohini_dutta21 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, you can configure approval workflows with amount thresholds for Supplier Invoices and Journal Entry Verification. However, it is not possible to create a standard workflow model that is triggered directly by KR/KG as the primary workflow object. It is not the standard design for Public Cloud.

For AP invoice process, I recommend you use Manage Workflows for Supplier Invoices and define start conditions by amount and any other attributes you require.

As an alternative option, you may use Manage Workflows for Supplier Invoices for FI journal governance with threshold-based conditions.

You can do your policy checks with validation or substitution but no as replacement for workflow approval.

Here is a quick guide:

Open Manage Workflows for Supplier Invoices,

either create or copy an existing workflow,

set your start conditions (company code, Amount threshold, etc), then maintain Approver steps and activate it.

then maintain Approver steps and activate it.

For FI journal governance with threshold-based conditions, you may create a workflow variant for amount thresholds, define your precondition and approver’s chain and activate it.

To summarise:

  • Standard flexible workflows for Supplier Invoices and Journal Entry Verification is the option for approval control by amount.
  • FI document types KR/KG is not the standard pattern.
  • Best practice is to implement threshold logic as mentioned above.

See the following references for more insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/b358966bcc3d4b68adedc3d817c74424.html?locale=en-US 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/3aa8e08e76184e69815a8fa9c7c1081a.html?locale=en-US 

These Blog have some good insights: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/configuring-exception-handling-in-a-verification-workflow/ba-p/13627633 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/how-to-create-workflows-that-require-multilevel-approval/ba-p/13535660 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Tax Reform Case
entry.content:
Question: To comply with the tax reform, two tax types (BR08 and BR09) were created to be assigned to the business partner.

 

The question is whether this information is only necessary to send the data to the calculation engine or if SAP uses it for any other purpose?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Tax Reform Case
entry.content:
Dear Customer

Thank you for your question regarding the tax types BR08 and BR09 created as part of the recent tax reform.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Cloud), these tax types assigned to the Business Partner are not used solely to send data to the tax calculation engine. While their primary purpose is to provide relevant information for tax calculation, they can also be used by the system for:

  • Tax determination logic during document processing, if included in the tax procedure and access sequences

  • Validation checks in documents such as billing or invoicing

  • Legal reporting and compliance processes, including electronic documents where applicable

For further details, you can refer to the official SAP documentation here:
Maintaining Tax Classification for Customers – SAP Help Portal

This document provides guidance on how tax classifications in Business Partner master data are maintained and used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Please let us know if you would like us to review your specific system configuration to confirm the usage of these tax types in your setup.

Best regards,
Aishwarya

If you want, I can also draft a shorter, more concise version suitable for faster customer reading. Do you want me to do that?

entry.title: One Plan, Multiple Measures: Driving Growth with Flexible Units in SAP Revenue Growth Management
entry.content:
1. When Units Don't Match: A KAM’s Silent Struggle 

Units-of-measure friction is a quiet but expensive problem for KAMs. Planning promotions in “cases” while accruals are tracked in “eaches” and analytics use “weight (e.g. KG)” creates a cycle of constant manual conversion, rework, and reconciliation across teams. The result: baseline volumes that don’t align with manufacturing plans, promotion uplifts that are misreported, and routine errors that slow decision cycles. Ultimately, these mismatches lead to lost time for Key Account Managers (KAMs) and business analysts, introduce audit risks for finance, and make collaboration with manufacturers and retailers harder than it should be. 

 

2. Consistency Across Every Unit and Role 

SAP Revenue Growth Management now supports Trade Units (TU), Consumer Units (CU), and Normalized Units (NU) across baseline setup, promotion planning, and analytics—with automated conversions based on your SAP material master data. The feature, Planning with Multiple Units of Measure (UoM), integrates planning inputs, execution records, and pre and post event analysis, enabling KAMs and business analysts to work from a single, consistent quantity model while each using the unit most relevant to their task. 

For more information on our current capabilities, see An Overview of SAP Revenue Growth Management. 

 

3. Key Functionalities of UoM in SAP Revenue Growth Management 

3.1. Flexible Units, Seamless Workflows: Planning Without Conversion Barriers   

Different customers, channels, and promotion types often require distinct units of measure. A bulk discount for a distributor may use pallets, while a Buy One, Get One (BOGO) promotion at retail may track individual consumer units. Without flexibility, KAMs face rigid workflows that don’t reflect business reality. 

 

Promotion Planning Tailored for Business Context

Instead of forcing all promotions into a single UoM, planning with multiple UoM within SAP Revenue Growth Management supports TU, CU, and NU concurrently. KAMs can now define promotion volumes using the most relevant UoM—whether it's cases (TU) for a warehouse shipment, individual bottles (CU) for a specific in-store display, or kilograms (NU) for a product sold by weight. This flexibility allows for promotions that precisely match customer requirements and channel dynamics. 

 

Automated, Error-Free Conversions

Manual UoM conversions often lead to rounding discrepancies or mismatched totals. Planning with multiple UoM ensures precision by automating conversions based on pre-defined rules from the SAP S/4HANA material master data. For example, a promotion planned for 1,000 Consumer Units (CU) is seamlessly reflected as the correct number of Trade Units (TU) for logistics and as Normalized Units (NU) for financial forecasting. This not only guarantees consistency across departments but also eliminates guesswork when translating promotional commitments into operational plans. 

 

3.2. End-to-End Process Integrity: From Accurate Forecasting to Seamless Execution 

A promotion's success hinges on the smooth flow of accurate information from initial planning stage all the way to the retailer's shelf. The flexibility of planning with multiple UoM is the glue that binds these stages together, providing clarity and efficiency for both KAMs and business analysts across the entire promotion lifecycle. 

 

Seamless Cross-Functional and Cross-System Alignment

This feature within SAP Revenue Growth Management bridges critical gaps between sales, supply chain, and finance teams. For instance, a KAM can plan a holiday promotion for a soft drink in CU ("2-liter bottles"). The system then seamlessly converts this into TU ("6-packs") for the manufacturer's production schedule and into NU ("liters") for unified profitability analysis. This end-to-end alignment reduces manual handoffs to manufacturing and logistics, significantly shortening the time from promotional agreement to product being shelf-ready. Furthermore, this seamless flow extends to integrated systems like demand planning software, ensuring data consistency across the tech stack without manual intervention. 

 

Deeper, Standardized Analytics and Reporting

With the ability to toggle between TU, CU, and NU across promotion planning, baseline, and analytics modules, planning with multiple UoM eliminates the data gaps that often obscure true promotion performance. Normalized Units (NU) provide a fair basis for cross-SKU and cross-category comparisons. For example, NU provides a fair basis for cross‑SKU and cross‑category comparisons (e.g., profit per liter or equivalent case), empowering analysts to uncover critical insights, such as trends in pack-size performance (e.g., family packs outperforming single-serve bottles during holidays) or to prioritize high-margin pack formats. This standardization also strengthens compliance, provides clear audit trails, and ensures consistent promotional reporting across the organization.  

 

3.3. Accelerate Time‑To‑Value with Out-of-the-Box Integration 

In a landscape where time-to-value is critical, the ability to leverage existing IT investments is a key advantage. The Planning with Multiple Units of Measure feature is designed for seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA, directly utilizing the material master data as the single source of truth. This native approach eliminates the need for complex custom mapping that often plagues solutions, setting it apart from systems that require extensive setup. 

 

Reduced Integration Complexity and Cost

Customers using SAP S/4HANA benefit from pre-configured mapping of material master data into SAP Revenue Growth Management, avoiding the manual creation of conversion tables and complex integration process. This translates to a significantly shorter deployment cycle and lower initial costs compared to building conversions from scratch or relying on third-party connectors that introduce additional layers of complexity and maintenance. 

 

Guaranteed Data Consistency and Integrity

By drawing on a single, authoritative source for master data, the risk of discrepancies from stitching together disparate systems is minimized. This foundation ensures that UoM conversions, which are pre-defined in the material master data, are applied consistently and accurately across all processes—from promotion creation to financial settlement—building trust in the data for reporting and compliance. 

 

4. Join Our Co-Innovation Initiative 

SAP Revenue Growth Management's capability for planning with multiple UoM tackles a major operational headache: it frees KAMs from manual conversions, delivers clearer volume signals to production and retail partners, and equips analysts with standardized metrics for robust, apples‑to‑apples comparisons. 

But this is just the beginning. We’re actively evolving the planning with multiple units of measure to offer even greater flexibility through configurable UoM rules. Imagine defining UoM preferences not just globally, but by individual account plan, specific customer, or trade channel—turning unit strategy into a competitive lever.  

If you manage accounts, lead revenue programs, or own trade promotion analytics in the CPG space, your expertise is vital to shaping what comes next. Contact us at RGM_Engage@sap.com to start collaboration. Let’s co-create the future and transform UoM challenges into a competitive advantage—together. 

 

More Information

Product Page for SAP Revenue Growth Management 

Documentation on SAP Help Portal

Topic Page for SAP Revenue Growth Management

    • Introducing Calendar View in SAP Revenue Growth Management: A Visual Leap for Strategic Planning
    • Decoding Promotional Success: Embedded Analytics in SAP Revenue Growth Management
    • Unlocking Trade Spend Control Efficiency with Funds Management in SAP Revenue Growth Management
    • Consolidating Customer Planning: Unleash Growth with SAP Revenue Growth Management’s Account Plan
    • Elevate Your Promotion Strategy: Baseline Management with SAP Revenue Growth Management 
    • Driving Promotion Excellence: Streamlined Creation and Copying with SAP Revenue Growth Management
    • Financial Execution & Claims for Trade Promotions with SAP Revenue Growth Management & SAP Cloud ERP
    • Master Your KPIs: How SAP Revenue Growth Management Boosts Data-Driven Growth 
    • Modernize Planning: Universal Hierarchy in SAP Revenue Growth Management for SAP Cloud ERP Customers
    • Promotion Creation Reimagined: AI‑Assisted Single & Bulk Workflows in SAP Revenue Growth Management
    • One Plan, Multiple Measures: Driving Growth with Flexible Units in SAP Revenue Growth Management

entry.title: SAP CPI: WSDL Headers Not Visible After Upload? Here’s the Fix
entry.content:
Introduction : When working with shared WSDLs in SAP CPI, SOAP headers are sometimes not fetched correctly after the WSDL upload. Even though the same WSDL works for both source and target systems, CPI may fail to display the headers. This blog explain a simple one-line fix to resolve the issue.

Body :

When performing message mapping in SAP CPI, it only considers elements defined inside the <schema> section of the WSDL.  Any SOAP headers that exist outside this scope—like custom or extended headers won’t be recognized by CPI. This can be confusing, because the WSDL may work perfectly in both your source and target systems, yet CPI doesn’t display the headers, making them impossible to map.

CPI parses only the schema-defined elements. Headers defined outside the schema or not explicitly referenced are ignored,

We need to add <xs:element ref="emf:EMFHeader" /> element to the source and target schema as shown in the shared screenshot. 

 

Step-by-Step Implementation

  1. Open the source schema in CPI.

  2. Locate the <xs:schema> section.

  3. Add <xs:element ref="emf:EMFHeader" /> at the appropriate position.

  4. Repeat the same process in the target schema.

  5. Save and reopen your mapping

After these steps, all previously hidden headers should be fully visible, ready for mapping.

Summary : In SAP CPI, SOAP headers may not appear in message mapping when using shared WSDLs because CPI only reads the elements defined in the schema. And adding <xs:element ref="emf:EMFHeader" /> to both source and target schemas makes the headers visible for mapping.

entry.title: Handling Job Info deletions using Compound Employee-Delta & Period Delta in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Handling Data Integrations in case of Deletion of Events in Job Information in SuccessFactors using Compound Employee-Delta and Period Delta Queries

Table of Contents

  • Summary
  • Business Scenario
  • Understanding Compound Employee Delta and periodDelta Queries
  • Employee Job History Timeline
  • Delta and periodDelta Query Execution and Observed Results
  • Why Deleted Job Records Appear in Delta and periodDelta Responses
  • Understanding *_previous Fields
  • Recommended Handling in SAP CPI
  • Conclusion

Summary

When querying SAP SuccessFactors Compound Employee using queryMode=delta and queryMode=PeriodDelta together with resultOptions=renderPreviousTags, deleted job records may still appear in the response. This document explains why this happens, how timestamps and previous tags are calculated, and how to correctly handle this behavior in SAP CPI or downstream systems.

Business Scenario

Organizations commonly integrate employee data from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to downstream systems using  delta queries. A frequent assumption is that deleted records should no longer appear in API responses.
However, when using Compound Employee in delta mode, integration developers may observe:

  • Deleted job records still being returned
  • *_previous fields showing unexpected values
  • Mismatches between UI state and API output

This document clarifies these behaviors using a real-world job information scenario.

Understanding Compound Employee Delta and periodDelta Queries

Compound Employee delta queries are driven by system modification timestamps (last_modified_on), not by the current UI state. Any change to a record - creation, update, or deletion - updates this timestamp and makes the record eligible for delta extraction.
This design ensures that downstream systems are aware of every change that occurs in SuccessFactors, including deletions. about this.

Employee Job History Timeline

The following timeline illustrates the employee job history used in this scenario.

EventEffective Start DateCost CenterNotes
New Hire1998-03-01South Africa Production (ZA01-4200)Initial job record
Job Change2026-01-30South Africa Procurement (ZA01-2300)Job change created
Future Job Record2026-02-05South Africa Production (ZA01-4200)Future-dated change created
Deletion2026-01-30South Africa Procurement (ZA01-2300)Job record deleted in UI


 Figure 1: Job Information in SuccessFactors UI before deletion

 Figure 2: Job Information in SuccessFactors UI after deletion

Delta and periodDelta Query Execution and Observed Results

The Compound Employee API was queried using delta mode with renderPreviousTags enabled and a last_modified_on timestamp greater than 2026-01-30T15:22:40Z.
Despite the deletion of the January 30 job record in the UI, the API response returned both the active job record and the deleted job record.

The Compound Employee API was queried using periodDelta mode with renderPreviousTags enabled and a last_modified_on timestamp greater than 2026-01-30T15:22:40Z and AND fromDate = to_date('2026-01-31','yyyy-MM-dd') AND toDate = to_date('2026-02-05','yyyy-MM-dd') AND person_id_external IN ('900103')
Despite the deletion of the January 30 job record in the UI, the API response returned both the active job record and the deleted job record.

Explanation of Job Information Records in Delta Response and periodDelta
The following The job_information records are returned as part of a Compound Employee delta query using queryMode=delta;resultOptions=renderPreviousTags along with the periodDelta. Even if one of the records has been deleted in the SuccessFactors UI, both records are included in the delta response to maintain historical consistency.

Active Job Information Record (Effective 2026-02-05)

<job_information>
    <action>CHANGE</action>
    <start_date>2026-02-05</start_date>
    <cost_center>ZA01-4200</cost_center>
    <cost_center_previous>ZA01-2300</cost_center_previous>
</job_information>

This record represents the currently effective Job Information after the deletion was performed. The employee’s cost center was reverted to ZA01-4200, effective from February 5, 2026.

The <cost_center_previous> tag is populated with ZA01-2300, indicating that the immediately preceding job record - before the effective-dated recalculation - had a different cost enter. This previous value reflects the system’s historical understanding of the job change, not necessarily the current UI state.

Deleted Job Information Record (Effective 2026-01-30)

<job_information>
    <action>CHANGE</action>
    <start_date>2026-01-30</start_date>
    <cost_center>ZA01-4200</cost_center>
    <cost_center_previous>ZA01-2300</cost_center_previous>
</job_information>

Understanding Job Event Information

Job Event Information Reflecting Insert, Delete, and Historical Job Events in a Compound Employee Delta Response

<job_event_information>
   <action>INSERT</action>
   <created_on_timestamp>2026-01-30T15:27:04.000Z</created_on_timestamp>
   <event>5</event>
   <event_date>2026-02-05</event_date>
   <event_reason>DATACHG</event_reason>
   <seq_number>1</seq_number>
</job_event_information>
<job_event_information>
   <action>DELETE</action>
   <created_on_timestamp>2026-01-30T15:20:40.000Z</created_on_timestamp>
   <event>5</event>
   <event_date>2026-01-30</event_date>
   <event_reason>DATACHG</event_reason>
</job_event_information>
<job_event_information>
   <action>INSERT</action>
   <created_on_timestamp>2017-01-19T18:36:54.000Z</created_on_timestamp>
   <event>H</event>
   <event_date>1998-03-01</event_date>
   <event_reason>HIRNEW</event_reason>
   <seq_number>1</seq_number>
</job_event_information>

This record corresponds to the Job Information entry that was deleted from the SuccessFactors UI. Even though it no longer exists in the front end, it is still delivered in the delta response because the deletion itself represents a change event.

SuccessFactors does not explicitly mark deleted job records with a DELETE action. Instead, the system recalculates the effective-dated job timeline and includes the deleted record in the delta output to allow downstream systems to understand the full sequence of changes that occurred.

Why the _previous Tag Appears for a Deleted Record

  • The _previous tag appears when:
  • renderPreviousTags is enabled in the query, and

A record is affected by an insert, update, or delete operation.

In this scenario, the _previous value (ZA01-2300) represents the cost center from the job record that existed before the recalculation triggered by the deletion. This ensures that consuming systems can reconcile changes correctly, even when historical records are removed.

Why Deleted Job Records Appear in Delta and periodDelta Responses

Deletion of a job record is treated as a system change. When a record is deleted, SuccessFactors updates its last_modified_on timestamp. Delta and periodDelta queries return this change to ensure downstream systems can react appropriately.

Additionally, SuccessFactors do not physically remove deleted records from the database. They are logically deleted to preserve audit history and data consistency.

Understanding *_previous Fields

The *_previous fields provided by renderPreviousTags represent historical comparisons between adjacent effective-dated records. These fields are history-aware, not UI-aware.
As a result, previous values may still reference deleted records if they were part of the job history at the time the change occurred.

Recommended Handling in SAP CPI For Delta Case Integration logic in SAP CPI must explicitly handle deleted job records. Common approaches include:

  • Processing only the latest active job record based on start_date
  • Avoiding renderPreviousTags when historical comparison is not required

How to Handle Deleted Job Records and Job Events in Integrations

Deleted Job Information records appearing in Compound Employee delta responses is a standard SuccessFactors behavior and must be handled through integration design, not UI assumptions. Since deletions are not always explicitly reflected in job_information, integrations should rely on a combination of delta semantics, job event actions, and effective-dated comparison logic.

Use Job Event Information as the Deletion Indicator

SuccessFactors explicitly communicates deletions through the job_event_information entity. When an event is removed from the system, it is delivered with an action = DELETE, even though the corresponding Job Information record may still appear in the delta payload.

Handling approach:

  • Identify job_event_information records with action = DELETE
  • Use event, event_date, and event_reason to correlate the deletion with previously processed job data
  • Mark the corresponding job records as deleted or end-dated in downstream systems

This is the most reliable way to detect deletions.

Do Not Rely on Job Information Action Alone

Job Information records related to deletions often appear with action = CHANGE. This does not indicate an update but reflects a timeline recalculation triggered by the deletion.

Best practice:

  • Treat Job Information delta records as state transitions
  • Avoid interpreting CHANGE as a simple field update
  • Always evaluate the record in relation to surrounding effective-dated entries

How I Would Handle the Aforementioned Best Practice Scenario in SAP CPI

In this scenario, an intermediate Job Information record effective 2026-01-30 was deleted after a newer Job Information record effective 2026-02-05 was created. As a result, the Compound Employee delta response contains both active and deleted job-related data, along with _previous values reflecting historical recalculation.

To handle this correctly in SAP CPI, the integration should follow the steps below.

Step 1: Identify the Latest Effective Job Record
For each employee, determine the currently effective Job Information by evaluating:

  • start_date
  • Effective date ordering

In this case:

  • The Job Information record with start_date = 2026-02-05 is treated as the active record.
  • This record should be upserted or retained in the target system.

Step 2: Detect the Deleted Job Record Using Job Event Information
Next, analyze job_event_information records:

  • Identify entries where action = DELETE
  • Match the event, event_date, and event_reason with job history already processed

If a Job Information record corresponds to a job_event_information where action = DELETE, the record is excluded from active processing.
Action in CPI:

  • Mark the Job Information record with start date 2026-01-30 as deleted or end-dated in the downstream system.
  • Do not re-create or re-upsert this record.

Step 3: Ignore Deleted Records During Job Information Processing
Although the deleted Job Information record still appears in the delta response:

  • It must not be treated as an active change
  • It must not be upserted again

Rule applied:

  • If a Job Information record corresponds to a deleted Job Event (as indicated by job_event_information with action = DELETE), the record is excluded from active processing.

Step 4: Use *_previous Fields Only for Context
The _previous fields (cost_center_previous) indicate historical values before the deletion-driven recalculation.

Step 5: Update Cache for the Next Delta Run
After processing:

  • Update the CPI cache or persistence layer with:
    • Active Job Information records
    • Logical deletion markers for removed records
  • Store the latest successful delta timestamp

This ensures that:

  • The deleted record is not reprocessed.
  • Future delta runs remain consistent.

Outcome of This Handling
By applying the above logic:

  • The active job record (2026-02-05) remains intact
  • The deleted record (2026-01-30) is correctly removed from active processing
  • Historical consistency and auditability are preserved

Duplicate or incorrect updates are avoided

Conclusion

Deleted Job Information records appearing in Compound Employee delta and periodDelta queries is an expected and intentional behavior of SAP SuccessFactors. Delta and periodDelta mode is designed to guarantee data consistency and historical accuracy, not to reflect only what is currently visible in the SuccessFactors UI. When renderPreviousTags is enabled, the API deliberately exposes historical transitions to help consuming systems understand how and why a record changed over time.

As illustrated in this scenario, deleting an intermediate Job Information record does not erase its impact on the employee’s job history. Instead, SuccessFactors recalculates the effective-dated timeline and delivers both the active and previously effective values through <*_previous> tags. This allows downstream systems to reconstruct the full sequence of changes, even when records are removed from the front end.

Understanding this behavior is critical for building reliable and predictable integrations. Middleware platforms such as SAP CPI must not assume that the absence of a record in the UI means it will be absent from delta responses. Instead, integrations should evaluate:

  • Effective start and end dates
  • Sequence and last-modified timestamps
  • Previous value tags in relation to the active record

By applying appropriate filtering, comparison, and reconciliation logic in SAP CPI, organizations can avoid misinterpreting deleted records as new or duplicate changes. This approach ensures accurate synchronization, preserves audit integrity, and aligns downstream systems with SuccessFactors as the system of record. This applies to Compensation Information in similar way.

Ultimately, a clear understanding of delta query semantics, effective dating, and historical recalculation behavior enables enterprise integrations that are resilient to retroactive corrections, deletions, and data realignments - common realities in HR master data management.

entry.title: Accelerating SAPUI5 Migration to BAS: A Practical Framework
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP has transitioned its development paradigm from SAP Web IDE to SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), pushing developers to migrate from legacy UI5 to Fiori applications to the new environment. This paper outlines a structured and scalable methodology to migrate SAP UI5 applications from WEB IDE to BAS, focusing on connectivity, tooling and configuration challenges.

Methodology:

  1. Log in to your BTP Account. Go into your space. Go to the Service Marketplace. Search for Business Application Studio. Open Business Application Studio Application.

 

Note: Pre-Requisite - Cloud Connector and Destination should be set up for connectivity with the S4.

2. Click on Create Dev Space. Give some Dev Space Name—select the SAP Fiori on the left. On the right side, under Additional SAP Extensions select HTML5 Application Template, Launchpad Module, Basic UI5 Template. Click on Create Dev Space.

3. Dev Space is created as shown below. It will take a few minutes to start. Once it's started, click on the Dev space. Here, I have named it “DemoMigration”. It will open the Development workspace. Bookmark this URL for future use and easy navigation to Workspace.

4. For the first time, click on the menu on the top left and select the open folder. Search for Projects Folder. Click on OK.

5. Click on Import on your Get Started page. It will prompt to start the migration. Select migration option. Below screen will appear.  

 

6. Select Destination and Hostname and Client Details.

Conclusion:

Migrating SAP UI5 applications from SAP Web IDE to SAP Business Application Studio is more than a technical upgrade. It represents a strategic change towards a cloud-native and future ready development environment aligned with that of SAP’s roadmap. This blog outlined a well-structured migration methodology, which includes prerequisite connectivity set up, dev space configuration, project import and environment preparation within BAS. By following these steps, organizations can ensure a smooth transition while minimizing disruption to existing Fiori applications. This structured approach simplifies migration from WEB IDE to BAS. It supports scalable and cloud-aligned SAPUI5 development for public institutions and enterprises.

.

entry.title: S/4 Hana - Track on SO schedule line level both requested delivery date and committed delivery date
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am working for a Make to Order scenario project, my customer wants to trace in the sales order just for statistical purpose at schedule line level both  the initial requested delivery date of the customer and also the committed date (if the date requested by the customer is not feasible).

In the sales order there is not availability check and are not configured lead times; the Customer care should enter manually the requested delivery date for each sales order item (also eventually splitting quantity on different dates), then if after discussion with Planning the date is not feasible the Customer care will enter for each schedule line previously defined the committed date (the date on which the company commits to the customer to deliver the product).

There is any standard solution to satisfy this requirement? Otherwise I am thinking to add a new custom field "committed date" on schedule line tab of the sales order.

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: SAP Signavio February 2026 Release: SAP Signavio Process Insights and Intelligence
entry.content:
Understanding how processes truly run — and turning those insights into action — remains one of the biggest challenges for organizations today. Teams often spend more time preparing data, interpreting results, or working around technical limitations than actually improving performance.

With this release, we’re focusing on removing that friction. Across SAP Signavio Process Insights and Intelligence, the latest innovations help you analyze processes more flexibly, uncover root causes faster, and work only with the data that matters. The result is simpler decision-making and quicker, more confident action.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

Model and analyze processes driven by business objects with Object-based data modeling

Real business processes rarely follow a single linear path. Orders, deliveries, invoices, and other business objects constantly interact, yet traditional case-based analysis often forces teams into rigid structures that capture only part of the story.

With object-based data modeling, you can now view these relationships in one unified model. By decoupling case logic from fixed data structures, you define your data model once and reuse it across multiple analyses, without duplicating or reshaping source data. This makes it far easier to explore how different parts of your business connect, shift perspectives depending on the question at hand, and adapt analyses as requirements evolve. Complex, end-to-end processes become more transparent and significantly easier to understand.

Object Based Data Modeling

Understand root causes and act faster with AI-assisted insights  (Beta)

Identifying performance issues is only the first step. Interpreting what’s driving them and and deciding what to fix first: this is where many teams lose time and momentum.

AI-assisted Root Cause Analysis helps bridge this gap by automatically translating analysis results into clear explanations and actionable recommendations. Instead of manually investigating dashboards or debating priorities, you receive contextual insights into why issues occur and which improvements are likely to deliver the greatest impact. This accelerates the journey from insight to action and helps ensure that improvement initiatives consistently focus on what matters most.

This capability is currently available in beta. If you’re interested in participating, we’d love to hear from you!

AI-assisted root cause analysis: Cause interpretation & next action recommendation

Interested to know more about Root Cause Analysis (RCA)? Check out our blog posts on RCA as well as RCA on event graphs!

SAP Signavio Process Insights

Use filtering to control what data is extracted for process analysis

Not every piece of ERP data is relevant for process performance analysis. Frankly, some information should never leave the source system at all. And, until now, teams often had limited control over what was extracted, leading to unnecessary data movement or additional governance work.

With enhanced filtering options, system owners can now exclude sensitive or irrelevant attributes directly within the extraction process. This allows organizations to better align with compliance requirements while also reducing data volume and focusing analyses on what truly adds value. The outcome is a more efficient, secure, and streamlined approach to process insights.

Explore process execution with interactive variants

First announced in the previous release (Nov. 2025), the Execution Variant (Previously Variant Explorer) is now available in SAP Signavio Process Insights, bringing a more interactive way to understand how processes actually are executed.

Embedded directly in the Process Flow page, it allows you to explore and compare process variants, filter for specific behaviors, and quickly see how different execution paths affect performance. With improved visuals, support for more variants, and the ability to intuitively include, exclude, and cluster flows, it’s now easier than ever to pinpoint inefficiencies and focus on the variants that matter most.

Together, these enhancements provide a clearer, execution-based view of your processes, this helping teams identify optimization opportunities faster and act with confidence.

Execution Variant

Click here to start a step-by-step enable now tutorial for this feature. 

Together, these updates are designed to make process analysis more adaptable, actionable, and aligned with real-world business needs, helping teams spend less time managing data and more time improving outcomes.

Want to know more about this release? Check out the highlight blog post and see what else we prepared for this release over the entire SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite! 

We are looking forward to seeing you on the SAP Signavio Community to share feedback, questions and discuss with your peers! This is your space! 

Please feel free to share your questions around the release or feedback in the comments with us. You can also reach out to us via the SAP Signavio Community or alternatively watch our Release Webcast on Demand.

entry.title: Auto-move/reject candidates with multiple applications
entry.content:
Overview:

I have heard this requirement a few times now, where the client would like to prevent candidates from applying for multiple requisitions/jobs. SuccessFactors does not prevent / restrict a candidate from applying to multiple requisitions, however, in this blog I will show how you can automatically move (or reject) candidates in the additional requisitions to a new status (1st application stays intact).

Pre-requisites:

  • Access to create a new field in the candidate profile (provisioning or Manage templates in Admin centre)
  • Access to create a new field in the application template (provisioning or Manage templates in Admin centre)
  • Access to business rules for recruiting

The solution:

In the candidate profile, create a custom text field (example: First Requisition Applied) to store the requisition id. Use the same field id to create the same field in the application. As both these fields are used for internal/technical purposes only, you do not have to worry about providing overrides or permissions.

See screenshot below:

Fig 1: Candidate profile XML showing the new field

 

Fig 2: Application XML showing the new field

Once this is done, create a new business rule under ‘Job Application: Basic Business Rule Scenario’, with the below condition (and save it under 'On Save Rules' in Manage rules in Recruiting).

1. If the newly created field ‘First Requisition Applied’ is NULL, then copy the requisition id to this field.

This ensures that if the candidate is applying for the first time, the field will be blank and hence we copy the requisition id , from the requisition into the field in the application (which in-turn automatically copies it into the candidate profile field as the field id’s are the same). For our example, let us assume the first requisition id, which the candidate applied to is 3086.

2. Else if the newly created field (First Requisition Applied) is NOT equal to the requisition id, then move the candidate to a new status (including any rejection statuses).

This ensures from the 2nd job application onward the candidate is automatically moved to the required status (including any rejection statuses). Example: the 2nd job has a requisition id as 3049, and when the candidate applies for this, the system checks if 3086 (from 1.) is NOT equal to 3049 and automatically moves the candidate.

Fig 3: Sample business rule:

 

Fig 4: Candidate profile screenshot

Once this rule is activated, every time a candidate clicks on 'Submit' for their application, this rule is triggered.

This way, with no major customisations or middleware being involved, we can change a candidate status (or reject) them on any additional job applications while still retaining the first application as-is.

Additional scenarios:

Parent - Child Requisition :

If the first requisition that the candidate is applying to is a parent requisition and you would be later moving the candidate to a child requisition, please ensure

  1. there is an application field that is populated after the ‘default’ status in the parent req
  2. this field needs to have the attribute ‘forward-intact=true’
  3. add this field to the business rule (an AND condition to 2. Above), where you ensure this field is blank.
  4. this way when the candidate is moved to the child req, this field will still be populated, and the business rule above will not auto-move or auto-reject the candidate
  5. Please see screenshot below with an example, where I am ensuring the ‘Contract country’ is blank, along with the requisition id check (as detailed in point 2 above)

Fig 5: Screenshot of the business rule when a common field is used to check for the parent-child relation:

Reset checks:

An additional requirement I’ve had is to reset the check on additional applications every year ie., in a year the candidate can only apply to one application, they can apply to a new job the next year / recruitment cycle.

  1. To meet this requirement, you can use Integration Centre to wipe out the field value in the candidate profile (not application – but Candidate profile – the source!), every year/cycle.

Things to note:

  • The field in the candidate profile can be hidden from the candidate view (by not adding any permissions). However, this field will be visible to internal recruiters / hiring managers and cannot be hidden. However, it can be re-labelled in a way no one gets confused (example ‘First Requisition applied’ OR ‘Technical field’ etc.,) with the use of the field along with some internal comms / change management
  • Last note, as this is a custom solution, SAP support will not help with any issues / debugging.

Please refer to other KBA’s and the community for all other Q and A’s.

entry.title: Re: India Vendor Return: Delivery Challan for Intrastate...
entry.content:
Purpose of the Delivery Challan app
The app (transaction J1IGINTRASTO in classic terms) is used to generate a Delivery Challan document for goods movements within India, including vendor returns. Even though you already post the return movement (122 or 161) in MIGO, Indian GST regulations require a delivery challan to accompany the physical movement of goods back to the vendor. The challan serves as a legal document for transport and tax compliance, not just an internal SAP posting. It contains details like vendor, plant, material, and billing type, which are mandatory for GST audit trails.2

Why it is required after MIGO
MIGO (movement type 122 or 161) creates the material document for the return. The Delivery Challan app then references that material document to generate the statutory challan. Without this step, you would have the stock movement recorded but no GST-compliant transport document, which could cause issues during audits or inspections.

Your error (“Customer name not maintained / No Bill To party defined”)
This usually indicates missing vendor master data configuration or billing type assignment for delivery challan. The app requires vendor details to be set up correctly in configuration (e.g., Bill-to party, GST registration details). Check the SSCUI or IMG activity for Configuration for Delivery Challan for Vendor Returns and ensure vendor and billing type are mapped.

Role when e-Way Bill integration (scope item 78L) is not in scope
Even if you generate e-Way Bills manually on the government portal, the Delivery Challan app is still required.

Reason: The challan is the basis for e-Way Bill generation. You need the challan details (document number, date, material, quantity, vendor) to enter into the government portal.

So, the app remains part of the compliant process, even without automatic integration.

entry.title: Re: SAP Service and Asset Manager Phase model Work Orders
entry.content:
Hi,
The Phase Model process normally starts with a 'maintenance request' , so a 'notification' in SSAM.
If you want to turn this into a Work Order immediately, skipping some of the intermediate phases, look into the creation of a notification 'with emergency processing context'. When you set this in SSAM, a Work Order will be created locally and be 'ready for execution'.
S/4 help guide on processing context: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e72f747389b340229f7fa343975bfa57/c9e3772f8ded4db4b91...
SSAM Config: https://help.sap.com/docs/service-asset-manager/sap-service-and-asset-manager-configuration-guide/em...
Hope this helps.
D.

entry.title: Re: Spanish EC Sales and Purchase List reporting
entry.content:
Good day @Jessicaaguilar1 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is a public cloud standard situation. It happens because, in Run Statutory Reports, the Manage Tax Items step is a preparation and review workspace for reporting period and company. It basically shows a broader tax item before you then finally see the specifics as you mentioned in your case. See KBA 3356046 for more insights.

So, the report data model/analyser then applies that report logic for ES_ECSL_DCL and keeps only records that meet EC list criteria for example intra EU relevant transactions.

Note that the two views are not inconsistent, they just display different processing layers below:

  1. Manage Tay Items = operational pool for review and inclusion control
  2. Report model output = legally determined dataset after report rules have been implemented.

I suggest you check the following in your system:

  • Verify that non-EC documents are either excluded automatically by report logic later or you can manually exclude them if you need to.
  • Confirm you selected the exact report ID ES_ECSL_DCL in Run Statutory Reports and correct your reporting entity/date.
  • The analyser will serve you as the validation point for final data scope, not the raw item list.

In summary, Spanish EC Sales and Purchase List, Manage Tay Items will display more invoices than the final EC report. The system applies the final filtering at the output stage, which is why your analyser correctly shows only intra EU relevant invoices.

See following references for more insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a624a4a6d1d8473eb95fb42660127cfe/aef42ea288a54ff3aa0ae38d8a4da203.html?locale=en-US&q=Making+Additional+Settings+for+VAT+Return+spain 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a624a4a6d1d8473eb95fb42660127cfe/7b31b01e5e4046fa9152620fc6df4898.html?locale=en-US&q=Making+Additional+Settings+for+VAT+Return+spain

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Questions on flexible workflow in purchase order
entry.content:
Q1 – Senior buyer creating a PO shouldn’t route to buyer approval
Flexible Workflow uses responsibility rules defined in the Teams and Responsibilities app. To prevent escalation downward, you need to configure the agent determination so that the workflow checks the creator’s role/limit before assigning approval.

Practically, this means:

Define responsibility rules by amount thresholds and role. Ensure the workflow condition excludes the creator if they already hold the authority for that threshold.
Example: If a Senior Buyer creates a PO ≤ 5000, the workflow can be configured to skip Buyer approval and either auto-approve or move to the next higher step.

Q2 – Senior buyer has left, workflow shouldn’t get stuck
If a team member leaves, the workflow can stall unless you maintain the team.

Options:

  • Maintain Teams and Responsibilities: Remove the departed member and assign a replacement.
  • Escalation/Alternate Approvers: Configure escalation paths or alternate approvers in the workflow definition so that if no agent is found, it routes to the next higher role (e.g., Manager).
  • Dynamic Agent Determination: Use rules that check availability; if no Senior Buyer is assigned, the workflow skips to Manager.

Q3 – Employee master data without HR integration
Yes, you can use the Maintain Employees app to create employee records in S/4HANA without SuccessFactors integration. Later, if you enable SuccessFactors Employee Central integration, you need to:

  • Map existing employee IDs to SuccessFactors IDs.
  • Perform a reconciliation so that workflow responsibilities continue to work seamlessly.
  • Typically, you’d migrate or align the employee master data so that the SuccessFactors feed becomes the single source of truth, but existing workflow teams can be adjusted to reference the new IDs.
