entry.title: Re: Manage Workflow for Purchase Requisitions app - skip...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your information.

I suppose you're looking for the following information, which is provided in the following SAP Help Portal document:

Manage Workflows for Purchase Requisitions

"You can choose to exclude restricted agents from being approvers of the purchase requisition. By default, the restricted agents are not excluded.

  • Exclude Requestors: By choosing this option, requestors or creators of purchase requisitions will not receive requisitions that are created by them for approval in their inbox.

  • Exclude Restricted Agents Determined by BAdI: By choosing this option, the agents set by the the BAdI Enhancement of Flexible Workflow Properties are excluded from being approvers of the workflow step.

After exclusion, if no recipients are determined, the workflow will move to error unless the step is marked optional.."

If this is not what you're looking for, please let us know.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: SmartForms: One Delivery Order with Multiple Customer POs Prints as Separate Invoices
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are facing the following scenario in SAP SD billing and invoice printing (SmartForms):

We have one Delivery Order, but the delivery contains items referencing multiple customer Purchase Order numbers. When billing is created and printed, SAP generates two separate invoices (different invoice numbers and separate print copies).

Business would ideally like the invoice to be printed as a single consolidated copy, even though there are multiple PO references.

We have verified that:

  • Same customer

  • Same Sales Order

  • Same Delivery Order

  • Same currency and payment terms

The only difference is the Customer PO number.

Questions:

  1. Is this standard SAP behavior (invoice split by customer PO)?
  2. Is there any standard configuration or SmartForms approach to prevent printing into two separate copies?
  3. If not, is enhancement the only option — and what are the usual considerations?

Thank you in advance for your advice.

System generates two invoice files from the same Delivery Order because the Customer PO number differs.

Field File A File B

Invoice No60320023046032002303
Delivery Order60100023276010002327
Sales Order134513134513
CustomerCustomer321Customer321
Payment TermsDue 30 DaysDue 30 Days
CurrencySGDSGD
Customer POSA02-1830102764SA02-1830088258

entry.title: How to unlink an identity from your SAP Universal ID
entry.content:
Learn how to unlink an identity (S-user ID/P-user) from your SAP Universal ID account. 

Steps on how to unlink an identity from an SAP Universal ID  
  
1. Sign into the SAP Account Manager.  

2. Navigate to the "Identities" section and locate the user to be unlinked.  

 

3. Select the "Unlink Identity" option from the three dots in the upper right corner of the chosen user.  

4. Click "Confirm" to complete the unlinking.  

 

 

You can also refer to KBA 3619621 How to unlink a user from an SAP Universal ID in SAP Account Manager? 

What to do if you encounter issues unlinking an identity 

If you cannot complete the above steps, please contact The Customer Interaction Center (CIC) by clicking here and provide the following details: 

  • Identity to be unlinked 
  • The reason for unlinking 
  • Proof of ownership (This request should be sent from the SAP Universal ID email address) 

You can find more details on SAP Universal ID deletion in this KBA 3539768 - How to delete an SAP Universal ID (UID)? or this; How to delete your SAP Accounts (P-User, S-User or Universal ID)  BLOG. 

Thanks for reading and I hope you found it useful!  

If you have any questions, please post in the Q&A (Question & Answer) section here: All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community     

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: Re: Display the attachedment directly in the web browser
entry.content: Yes, it’s possible, but it depends on the file type and server settings. Files like PDFs or images can open directly in the browser if the server sends them with the header Content-Disposition: inline instead of attachment. If your PR system enforces downloads for security, you’ll need to check with the admin or vendor to see if inline display is supported.

entry.title: Re: 10 reasons to trust the SAP Business Transformation Center with your data migration
entry.content:
Thank you for your questions @Atul_Joshi85 . Gives me the opportunity to add some thoughts and infos.

1. SDT versus Greenfield: Speaking personally, many Greenfield programs struggle: they take too long, cost a lot, and risk delivering processes that are already outdated by go-live. For many customers, it is more practical to get to the current platform (SAP S/4HANA) quickly with Selective Data Transition (SDT), then improve iteratively. The era of rolling out a template and leaving it unchanged for 10 years is over. My hope is that SAP BTC lowers the barrier for faster SDT migrations, so customers can move now and evolve continuously.

2. Clean Core and footprint reduction: The “cutoff date” depends on several factors (compliance, audit, finance, operations). SAP BTC helps by showing fact-based footprint reduction for different dates, so stakeholders can weigh benefits and trade-offs. In some cases, laws require certain data to stay in the S/4 system. It’s also important to stress that data is not “lost” if it is not migrated. With a proper decommissioning plan for ECC and, ideally, an ILM strategy, teams can still access older data when needed. Without filling S/4 with unnecessary history.

3. Security, residency, and the cloud: A careful approach to sensitive data is a core product standard for SAP BTC and SAP Cloud ALM in general. Features undergo explicit security reviews and audits. Explaining our architecture has already helped to convince “cloud-hesitant” stakeholders. 

4. Skills gap:  SAP BTC is not an expert-tool. Functional consultants and IT teams can use it using the guided, Fiori-based UX. You should still involve someone with Basis skills—especially for initial setup (onboarding ABAP systems ect.) and performance tuning during execution. But we also provide strong support for these steps. E.g. the Cloud Integration Automation Service (CIAS) for setup and a recently published Performance Guide for optimization.

5. Real-world content and edge cases: So far, the predefined BTC content has worked well in our projects and go-lives. Maybe als worth mentioning is that also BTC content can be updated daily, which lets us react quickly if issues arise. For truly unique situations, our Support has helped address customer-specific needs.

entry.content:
I would like to inquire about a specific issue regarding stock status. Currently, our inventory is in "Quality Inspection" status, but there is no corresponding quality inspection lot available, which prevents us from using transaction QA32.

We need to change the stock status from "Quality Inspection" to "Unrestricted." Could you please advise on the steps required to transfer this inventory to an unrestricted status?

We attempted to use MIGO to perform a 321 inspection in order to change the stock status to Unrestricted, but we encountered an error message stating, "You can only change the inspection stock for material XXXXXXX in Quality Management.(只有在品質管理中更改物料XXXXXXXXXX檢驗庫存)"

entry.title: Re: India Process Ewaybill for PO Return Delivery Questio...
entry.content: You Purchase Orders cannot be used for e‑Way Bill generation. GST requires a tax document (invoice, bill of supply, or delivery challan). If the vendor has not yet issued the invoice at the time of pickup, they can issue a delivery challan instead. This delivery challan serves as the reference document for you (buyer) to generate the e‑Way Bill. Later, when the invoice is issued, it becomes the commercial/tax document for accounting, but the challan covers the transport compliance at the time of pickup.

entry.title: Is it supported to map a master‑data custom field into the payment data / outgoing payment file?
entry.content:
We would like to confirm the following at a high level for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

  1. Feasibility
    Is it supported to map a master‑data custom field (e.g., on Supplier) into the payment data / outgoing payment file?

  2. If supported, are these steps correct?
    a) Add the “append” field to the payment data source
    App: Create Payment Medium Formats (CBC) → Including Custom Reference Fields (e.g., ZREFxx)
    b) Populate the appended field with logic
    App: Custom Fields and Logic (implement BAdI “Payment Medium: Custom Additional Reference Fields” / Event 06)
    c) Map the appended field to the DMEE‑equivalent format mapping
    App: Map Format Data for Payments (and assign the mapping in Deviating Format Tree, if applicable)

entry.title: SAP Signavio value accelerators: what’s new in the February 2026 release
entry.content:
Uncover, optimize, and accelerate enterprise transformation with SAP Signavio’s value accelerators February 2026 Innovations 

Delivering transformation at speed requires both clarity and actionable insight. This latest release of SAP Signavio value accelerators empowers transformation leaders, process consultants, and business and IT teams to align more closely with SAP best practices, increase transparency, and drive measurable performance improvements across core business processes. 

From enhanced process architectures and best-practice models to powerful new capabilities in process and operational intelligence, this release equips you to accelerate fit-to-standard assessments, improve automation and data quality, strengthen governance, and optimize end-to-end execution. Whether you are standardizing processes, improving productivity, reducing risk, or enabling enterprise-wide transformation, SAP Signavio helps you unlock value faster, at scale, and with confidence. 

Read on to explore what’s new for this February: 

 

Value Accelerator Library 

Discovery of Value Accelerators based on Value Drivers 

Business users and transformation leads can now easily discover and explore available value accelerators based on the value they deliver for specific lines of business and use cases. By connecting accelerators to clear value drivers, this feature helps users identify the most suitable options, understand their potential impact, and foster stronger communication and alignment between business and transformation teams around shared value goals. 

 

ERP & Business Transformation 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to remove data objects with no transactional usage 

Designed for IT professionals and transformation leaders focused on improving data quality, accelerating migrations, and reducing data management costs, this value accelerator in SAP Signavio Process Insights delivers fast, in-depth insights into unused data objects within SAP ERP systems. By highlighting objects with no transactional usage across areas such as Finance and Supply Chain, the dashboard enables teams to act on clear recommendations, streamline data usage and migration, uncover high-impact clean-up opportunities, and systematically improve master data quality, all supported by expert guidance grounded in decades of SAP consulting experience. 

 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to reduce the number of customer-specific configuration 

Built for IT professionals and transformation leaders seeking to increase adherence to standardized SAP business processes and accelerate migration timelines, this dashboard in SAP Signavio Process Insights provides fast, in-depth insights into customer-specific configuration objects within SAP ERP systems. By delivering actionable recommendations, particularly in areas such as sales, this feature helps teams reduce custom configurations, strengthen process standardization, identify high-impact improvement opportunities, and prioritize efforts effectively, all while benefiting from expert guidance shaped by decades of SAP consulting experience. 

 

Process design and standardization for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025-FPS0 

This value accelerator empowers transformation leaders and process consultants to align with SAP best practices while effectively modeling “to be” processes for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025-FPS0. By leveraging SAP’s best-practice business process models within SAP Signavio Process Modeler, teams can accelerate fit-to-standard assessments, deepen process understanding, and support a smooth transition to version 2505. With structured, up-to-date process models, organizations can strengthen compliance with SAP standards, simplify “as-is” and “to-be” comparisons, and make more informed decisions through clearer, more consistent process design. 

 

Process landscape design including RBA and S/4HANA best practices optimized for integrated toolchain 

This enhanced value accelerator empowers transformation leaders and process consultants to easily navigate the relationships between the SAP Reference Architecture and SAP S/4HANA best practices through a clear, harmonized content structure. By delivering transparent mapping from high-level process hierarchies to detailed solution processes, it removes ambiguity, accelerates decision-making, and supports integrated toolchain use cases through seamless synchronization with SAP LeanIX. With a unified source of truth for business and IT stakeholders, this feature strengthens process understanding, improves alignment, simplifies ERP transformation efforts, and increases adherence to SAP standard processes. 

Supply Chain 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to improve on-time delivery performance in process manufacturing 

Designed for manufacturing professionals, this dashboard in SAP Signavio Process Insights helps improve on-time delivery performance in process manufacturing by revealing how reliably process order components are confirmed and delivered according to their required dates. By highlighting gaps and opportunities in component confirmation and availability, the dashboard enables teams to increase confirmation rates, reduce stock-outs, and ultimately strengthen on-time delivery performance across manufacturing operations. 

 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to improve on-time delivery performance in discrete manufacturing 

Designed for manufacturing professionals, this dashboard in SAP Signavio Process Insights enhances on-time delivery performance in discrete manufacturing by showing how consistently production order components are confirmed and delivered according to their required dates. By identifying risks and improvement areas in component availability and fulfillment, the dashboard helps teams increase confirmation rates, reduce stock-outs, and strengthen overall on-time delivery across manufacturing operations. 

 

Sales 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to improve on-time delivery performance in sell from stock 

Designed for sales order management professionals, the Improvement Opportunity dashboard in SAP Signavio Process Insights improves on-time delivery performance in sell-from-stock scenarios by showing how consistently sales order items are confirmed and delivered according to customer-requested dates. By highlighting risks related to availability and fulfillment, the dashboard helps teams increase confirmation rates, reduce stock-outs, and strengthen overall on-time delivery performance. 

 

Finance 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to reduce credit management cost 

Designed for credit management professionals in Accounts Receivable, the Improvement Opportunity dashboard in SAP Signavio Process Insights helps reduce credit management costs by highlighting how often credit blocks occur and the reasons behind them. By providing clear visibility into blocking patterns and risks, the dashboard enables teams to lower credit exposure, streamline review efforts, minimize delays in the order-to-cash process, and accelerate the release of credit blocks without increasing financial risk.

 

Reduce duplicate payments for Invoice to Pay in SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP Central Component 

Built to support finance professionals facing challenges in identifying duplicate payments, this value accelerator in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence delivers clear, actionable insights into payment risks across SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP Central Component. By uncovering root causes, flagging high-risk cases, and highlighting erroneous or redundant supplier payments, this solution minimizes unwanted transactions, reduces manual investigation efforts, and enables faster recovery of funds, while strengthening internal controls, lowering operational risk, and ensuring a smooth, uninterrupted payment process. 

 

Reduce duplicate invoices for Invoice to Pay in SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP Central Component 

Built to support finance professionals facing challenges in identifying duplicate invoices, this value accelerator in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence provides clear visibility into root causes and high-risk cases that require additional review. By helping teams detect and prevent duplicate invoices before payment, this solution minimizes unnecessary financial risk, reduces manual clearing efforts, and strengthens internal controls to lower overall operational risk. 

 

Procurement 

Process flow and PPI analysing changes in scheduling agreements in SAP ERPs 

Built to support logistics and procurement planners, this value accelerator helps uncover root causes of delayed or incomplete inbound deliveries, such as short-notice call-off changes triggered by MRP runs or manual updates. By increasing transparency into these disruptions, the solution enables more reliable ordering and delivery, strengthens supplier and customer relationships, improves production efficiency through reduced supply volatility, and lowers working capital requirements by minimizing the need for buffer inventory. 

 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to drive operational procurement FTE productivity 

Created to support procurement professionals facing low operational FTE productivity, this dashboard in SAP Signavio Process Insights delivers fast, in-depth insights into common business challenges such as low purchase requisition and purchase order automation rates. By making complex issues easy to understand, even for first-time users and non-process experts, the improvement opportunity dashboard helps teams save time on analysis, gain a clear overview of productivity barriers, and act on targeted recommendations to improve efficiency and boost overall FTE performance. 

 

Identify improvement potential for procure to pay in SAP S/4HANA 

Built to support procurement excellence and transformation leaders, this value accelerator in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence serves as a central entry point for uncovering and realizing performance improvements across the procure-to-pay process in SAP S/4HANA. By delivering continuous transparency into key performance indicators and process variants, enabling benchmarking and prioritization of high-impact opportunities, and identifying concrete savings potential, this accelerator helps streamline operations, reduce costs, increase profitability, and foster stronger collaboration and trust with suppliers and internal stakeholders. 

 

Improve process performance for procure to pay in SAP ERPs 

Designed to support procurement excellence and transformation leaders, this value accelerator in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence provides targeted guidance to increase automation rates for purchase requisitions and purchase orders. Through focused analysis of manually created requisitions, manual PR-to-PO conversions, and purchase orders without requisitions, this solution helps teams pinpoint root causes of manual processing, improve requisitioner satisfaction, reduce handling costs, and shorten overall process cycle times. 

 

Identify improvement potential for procure to pay in SAP ERP Central Component 

Built to support procurement excellence and transformation leaders, this value accelerator in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence serves as a central starting point for uncovering value and improving procure-to-pay performance in SAP S/4HANA. By delivering enhanced transparency through continuous KPI and process-variant monitoring, enabling benchmarking and prioritization of high-impact opportunities, and identifying concrete savings potential through targeted initiatives, it helps streamline operations, reduce costs, increase profitability, and foster trust and collaboration with suppliers and requisitioners through more efficient, accurate, and timely purchase order processing. 

 

Conclusions 

The latest SAP Signavio release further strengthens how organizations plan, execute, and optimize their transformation initiatives. With enhanced value accelerators, improved opportunity dashboards, richer process architectures, and expanded analytics across ERP & Transformation, Finance, Procurement, Sales and Supply Chain, this release delivers the clarity, transparency, and actionable insight needed to drive transformation success. 

By combining SAP best-practice content with powerful capabilities from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and Process Insights, transformation leaders can better align strategy with execution, uncover high-impact improvement opportunities, and achieve measurable business outcomes. From accelerating standardization and automation to enabling continuous performance improvement, SAP Signavio empowers organizations to transform with confidence and deliver lasting value. 

To check out all the new accelerators mentioned in this blog post, access the value accelerator library, which is embedded into SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite. Find the value accelerator library in the side panel of SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub and get started today. 

If you’re interested in learning more about the rest of the SAP Signavio solutions, check out the blog posts below:  

 

  • SAP Signavio February 2026 Release Highlights   
  • Process Analysis and Mining   
  • Process and Journey Modeling 
  • New AI capabilities and enhancements   
  • Process Transformation Management and Collaboration   
  • Process Governance

entry.title: SAP Signavio Process and Journey Modeling: what's new in the February 2026 release
entry.content:
SAP Signavio continues to advance usability, intelligence, and collaboration with the February release, empowering organizations to model processes faster, improve accuracy, and strengthen transparency across their process landscape. The innovations outlined below enable process modelers and analysts to transform textual inputs into high-quality BPMN diagrams, automatically enrich models with AI-generated documentation, analyze simulation results instantly, and gain clearer visibility into decision logic directly within the SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub. 

With AI-powered modeling enhancements and an improved decision table preview experience with dictionary support, this release reinforces SAP Signavio’s commitment to intelligent automation, clarity, and seamless collaboration. Together, these capabilities reduce manual effort, enhance modeling quality, and equip organizations to drive data-informed process improvements with greater speed, confidence, and precision. 

Here is a summary of what the new February release brings to you: 

 

AI-assisted process modeler enhancements 

The latest AI-assisted enhancements to the process modeler help teams create more accurate, insightful, and easier-to-understand BPMN models. By combining intelligent diagram generation with automated documentation, modelers can now capture both high-level context and detailed task information without adding complexity or manual effort. 

 

  • Create concise, AI-powered process summaries and short task descriptions from your textual process input and store as a default "documentation" attribute allowing, which preserves all contextual information without cluttering the diagram or compromising readability. 
  • Automatically generate more complex BPMN diagrams with higher accuracy, derived by an upgraded large language model and improved technical architecture. Enhanced semantic understanding, smarter sentence parsing, and improved mapping of organizational roles to BPMN pools and lanes significantly reduce manual corrections and increase modeling quality.
  • Empower process analysts with instant insights into simulation results. Instead of navigating to separate simulation pages, users can review AI-generated summaries of bottlenecks, costs, and cycle times directly alongside their diagrams. This enables faster analysis, more informed decision-making, and optimized resource allocation, supporting continuous process improvement with minimal effort.

 

Enhance the Decision Table View in Collaboration Hub with dictionary support 

SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub users can now preview decision tables directly within the Hub alongside their process content, making it easier than ever to understand decision logic without switching tools or views. This new feature enhances clarity, supports stronger team alignment through shared visibility, and improves transparency by giving stakeholders a centralized space to review and understand how decisions are made. 

 

 

 

Conclusions 

The February release reinforces SAP Signavio’s commitment to delivering intelligent, AI-powered capabilities that simplify process modeling, enhance transparency, and strengthen collaboration across the enterprise. By advancing automated documentation, improving modeling accuracy, enabling instant simulation insights, and expanding visibility into decision logic, this release helps organizations work more efficiently, maintain alignment, and drive continuous improvement. Together, these enhancements support a more integrated, insight-driven approach to transformation, enabling enterprises to reduce complexity, accelerate change, and lead with confidence in an increasingly dynamic business environment. 

If you’re interested in learning more about the rest of the SAP Signavio solutions, check out the blog posts below:   

  • SAP Signavio February 2026 Release Highlights   
  • Process Analysis and Mining   
  • Value Accelerators 
  • New AI capabilities and enhancements   
  • Process Transformation Management and Collaboration   
  • Process Governance

entry.title: Simplify SAP Signavio Lab Space Beta Testing with the New Framework Test and Evaluation Agreement
entry.content:
Changing How You Test SAP Innovations

With our February 2026 minor release, we are excited to announce a small but impactful change that will make your life as an SAP Signavio user easier. Our goal is to help you get involved with upcoming beta features faster, by simplifying the beta access process.

 

What is the Framework Test and Evaluation Agreement (FTEA)?

The FTEA is a single, centralized agreement for beta testing—a one-time, all access pass to beta features for your organization. Once accepted, you can participate in any standard beta program across SAP without the need to sign additional agreements.

 

What Signing This New Agreement Means for You

Get Instant Access to More Betas:
When a new beta feature appears in the SAP Signavio Lab Space, you can join immediately – without waiting on additional legal reviews.

Reduce Administrative Work:
Your teams can stop spending time on repetitive paperwork and focus on hands-on evaluation and validation of new technology.

Make Your Voice Heard More Often:
A simpler process makes it easier for your teams to provide consistent feedback, helping us continuously improve and shape  the products you need.

 

How to Get Started

Activating the FTEA for your company is a simple, one-time process.

Step 1: Initiate the Agreement
You can start the process in one of two ways:

  • In the SAP Signavio Lab Space UI: Log in to your SAP Signavio Lab Space account and click either the 'Get Full Access' or 'Enable Application' button.
  • Via Email: Contact the SAP Signavio Lab Space team at signavio_lab_space@sap.com with your SAP Signavio workspace ID and ask about the FTEA process.

Step 2: Review and Accept the FTEA
Review the terms and accept them. This is a single action that allows your organization to test future beta features.

Step 3: Explore Beta Programs
That’s it! Once the FTEA is active, you'll be able to access to the full range of beta programs. You can start exploring the current beta features right away, on the SAP Signavio Lab Space homepage.

The introduction of the FTEA marks a meaningful improvement in easily accessing beta features. By replacing individual agreements with a single framework agreement, you can focus on testing new betas earlier and providing the feedback that helps shape the future of SAP Signavio.

Log in to SAP Signavio Lab Space or email the SAP Signavio Lab Space team to get started with the FTEA today.

entry.title: Re: S/4 Hana - Track on SO schedule line level both reque...
entry.content:
In standard SAP S/4HANA (Private Cloud), the sales order schedule line already has the field for Requested Delivery Date (customer request). What it does not have out‑of‑the‑box is a second “committed date” field for statistical comparison, unless you are using ATP/availability check where the system proposes a confirmed date automatically. Since in your scenario ATP is not active and lead times are not configured, there is no standard field that captures a manually agreed “committed date.”

Standard options

Requested Delivery Date: maintained manually by Customer Care at schedule line level.
Confirmed Delivery Date: normally populated by ATP check. If ATP is switched off, this field is not filled automatically.
Delivery Schedule functionality: allows multiple schedule lines with different requested dates, but does not provide a separate “committed” field unless ATP is active.

Implication
Without ATP, the “Confirmed Delivery Date” field remains blank. There is no standard mechanism to manually enter a committed date distinct from the requested date.

Possible approaches

Custom field on schedule line
Add a custom field “Committed Date” via In‑App Extensibility (Custom Fields and Logic). Place it on the schedule line tab of the sales order. This allows Customer Care to manually record the agreed date after discussion with Planning. You can then report statistically on requested vs. committed dates.

Use existing Confirmed Date field manually
Technically, you can allow manual entry into the “Confirmed Delivery Date” field if ATP is not active. However, this is not standard practice and may require enhancement, because SAP expects this field to be system‑driven. If you want clear separation between “requested” and “committed,” a custom field is cleaner.

Reporting layer workaround
Capture the committed date in a separate Z‑table or via notes, then expose it in CDS views for reporting. Less user‑friendly, but avoids modifying the sales order UI.

entry.title: Re: Behind the Curtains - what's new about Maintenance Certificates
entry.content:
Hello @guentherb,

1) moving the federation lead role is currently not supported.

2) yes, in the upcoming PL70 there will be a new function "Reset" where you can reset the federation role. However, please be aware that performing this action on a lead will dissolve the entire federation while on a peer it will only remove the peer from its federation. There will also be warnings in the UI 😉

3) as for your current issue, now there is no way to demote a lead to a peer, but with PL70 you can reset the lead's status and then add it as a peer to another federation.

BR QiAn

        stat_rate            = 0

*          RETURNCODE = 0.
*        WHEN 1.
*          MESSAGE ID 'SDBW' TYPE 'A' NUMBER '005'
*                  WITH COMM_STRUCTURE-doc_number

*                       COMM_STRUCTURE-loc_currcy.

      EXPORTING
        date                 = COMM_STRUCTURE-/bic/ybeldat

        document_currency    = 'EUR'
        local_currency       = 'RMB'

        stat_rate            = 0

      EXCEPTIONS

        stat_rate_not_found  = 2.
    CASE sy-subrc.
      WHEN 0.

        RETURNCODE = 0.
      WHEN 1.
        MESSAGE ID 'SDBW' TYPE 'A' NUMBER '005'
                WITH COMM_STRUCTURE-doc_number
                     COMM_STRUCTURE-/bic/ybeldat
                     COMM_STRUCTURE-doc_currcy
                     COMM_STRUCTURE-loc_currcy.
        RETURNCODE = 4.
      WHEN 2.
        MESSAGE ID 'SDBW' TYPE 'A' NUMBER '006'
                WITH COMM_STRUCTURE-doc_number
                     COMM_STRUCTURE-/bic/ybeldat
                     COMM_STRUCTURE-doc_currcy
                     COMM_STRUCTURE-stat_curr.
        RETURNCODE = 4.

    ENDCASE.
*    ELSE.
*      RESULT_TABLE-net_val_s = COMM_STRUCTURE-/bic/yzpre.
*    ENDIF.

    IF COMM_STRUCTURE-doc_categ CA deb_cred.
      RESULT_TABLE-net_val_s = RESULT_TABLE-net_val_s * ( -1 ).
    ENDIF.
    APPEND RESULT_TABLE.
  ENDIF.

 

Regards

RG

entry.title: SAP TM Lock Trace Analysis - Unlocking Performance Insights with Internal Support Tool
entry.content:
Introduction

Transportation Management systems handle complex, multi-user scenarios where freight orders, transportation requirements, and planning operations compete for the same resources. When multiple users simultaneously access TM documents, lock conflicts can create bottlenecks that impact your entire logistics operation. Understanding and resolving these conflicts is crucial for maintaining optimal system performance.

Lock tracing in SAP TM provides the visibility needed to identify, analyze, and resolve these performance-critical issues before they escalate into business disruptions. In this comprehensive guide, we'll explore how to leverage SAP TM's specialized lock tracing tools to maintain peak system performance and ensure smooth transportation operations.

What Are SAP TM Lock Traces?

Lock tracing in Transportation Management captures and analyzes optimistic lock creation events for TM business objects, providing detailed insights into when, where, and why lock conflicts occur. Unlike traditional database locks, TM's optimistic locking mechanism allows multiple users to read data simultaneously but prevents conflicting updates to the same transportation documents.

Lock conflicts in TM typically arise when multiple users attempt to modify freight orders, transportation requirements, or planning scenarios simultaneously. These conflicts can manifest as extended wait times, process delays, or even deadlock situations that prevent critical transportation operations from completing.

The business impact extends beyond mere system performance - lock conflicts can delay freight order processing, disrupt transportation planning cycles, and ultimately affect customer service levels and operational efficiency.

Meet Your Lock Tracing Toolkit

Report /SCMTMS/TMLOCKTRACE02 - The Data Collector

This specialized diagnostic program serves as your primary data collection engine for TM lock analysis. TMLOCKTRACE02 monitors and captures lock creation events using checkpoint group /SCMTMS/LOCKING, storing detailed information about lock conflicts and blocking scenarios in database table /SCMTMS/D_LOG.

The program excels at identifying blocking processes, analyzing wait conditions between competing operations, and providing the raw data needed for comprehensive lock conflict analysis. It's designed to run during normal TM operations without significantly impacting system performance.

Key capabilities include real-time lock event monitoring, conflict detection between competing processes, and comprehensive data collection for subsequent analysis.

/SCMTMS/TMLOCKTRACE03 - The Analysis Engine

While TMLOCKTRACE02 collects the data, TMLOCKTRACE03 transforms that raw information into actionable insights. This companion program displays collected lock trace logs in a user-friendly format, enabling administrators to quickly identify patterns, assess performance impacts, and develop targeted resolution strategies.

The analysis engine complements the data collector by providing visualization capabilities, trend analysis, and detailed reporting on lock-related performance issues. Together, these tools form a complete lock monitoring and analysis solution for TM environments.

Step-by-Step Implementation Guide

Setting Up Lock Trace Monitoring

  1. Access the Program: Execute /SCMTMS/TMLOCKTRACE02 via SE38, SE80, or SA38.

  1. Configure Parameters: Set up trace parameters including time ranges, user filters, and specific TM business object selection
  2. Activate Monitoring: Enable the lock trace to begin collecting data during normal TM operations
  3. Verify Setup: Ensure the trace is properly activated and collect data before proceeding

Best practices include focusing on specific business objects during initial setup, defining appropriate time windows for data collection, and ensuring sufficient system resources for trace operations.

Collecting Meaningful Data

Optimal collection periods should align with peak TM usage times when lock conflicts are most likely to occur. Monitor freight order processing, transportation planning cycles, and periods of high concurrent user activity.

During collection, observe system performance indicators and note any user-reported delays or issues. This contextual information will prove valuable during analysis.

Common pitfalls include running traces for insufficient duration, failing to capture peak usage periods, and not coordinating with business users to understand operational patterns.

Analyzing Your Results

  1. Access Analysis Tool: Execute /SCMTMS/TMLOCKTRACE03 to display collected trace logs.

  1. Review Data: Examine lock events, wait times, and blocking relationships
  2. Identify Patterns: Look for recurring conflicts, specific document types with frequent locks, or users experiencing consistent issues
  3. Assess Impact: Evaluate how lock conflicts affect overall system performance and business operations

If no traces appear despite activation, the trace log table may have exceeded its 500-entry limit. Use program /SCMTMS/DELETE_LOG to clear old entries and enable new trace collection.

Real World Scenarios

Scenario 1: Freight Order Processing Bottlenecks

Problem: Multiple planners attempting to modify the same freight orders during peak processing hours, causing delays in order fulfillment.

Detection: TMLOCKTRACE02 reveals multiple competing processes accessing identical freight order documents, with extended wait times during specific time periods.

Resolution: Implement process changes to distribute workload, adjust user access patterns, or modify business processes to minimize concurrent access to the same documents.

Scenario 2: Transportation Planning Conflicts

Symptoms: Planning optimization runs taking significantly longer than expected, with users reporting system unresponsiveness during planning cycles.

Analysis: Trace data shows lock conflicts between planning processes and manual freight order modifications, creating cascading delays.

Optimization: Establish dedicated planning windows, implement process coordination between planning and operational teams, and optimize planning scenarios to reduce lock duration.

Scenario 3: System-Wide Performance Degradation

Business Impact: Overall TM system performance declining during business hours, affecting multiple transportation processes simultaneously.

Detection: Comprehensive trace analysis reveals circular lock dependencies between multiple TM processes, creating deadlock scenarios.

Prevention: Implement process sequencing, optimize database access patterns, and establish monitoring procedures to detect similar issues proactively.

Pro Tips for TM Administrators

Monitoring Best Practices

Establish regular trace execution schedules during peak TM operations to maintain baseline performance metrics. Implement proactive monitoring approaches that identify potential issues before they impact business operations.

Create performance baselines during normal operations to quickly identify anomalies and establish thresholds for automated alerting when lock conflicts exceed acceptable levels.

Troubleshooting Common Issues

Empty Trace Results: Verify that the checkpoint group /SCMTMS/LOCKING is properly activated and that TM operations are generating lock events during the monitoring period.

Log Table Overflow: When the /SCMTMS/D_LOG table exceeds 500 entries, new traces cannot be created. Regular cleanup using /SCMTMS/DELETE_LOG prevents this limitation from affecting ongoing monitoring.

Performance Impact: If trace activation affects system performance, consider adjusting trace parameters or scheduling collection during lower-usage periods.

Integration with Other Tools

Complement lock trace analysis with SM12 for real-time lock overview and immediate lock release when necessary. Use ST12 for integrated performance tracing when lock conflicts correlate with broader performance issues.

Leverage checkpoint group analysis through transaction SAAB to examine related TM performance indicators and gain comprehensive insights into system behavior.

Key Takeaways

Lock trace analysis provides essential visibility into TM performance bottlenecks, enabling proactive identification and resolution of conflicts before they impact business operations. Regular monitoring during peak usage periods helps maintain optimal system performance and user productivity.

Implementation should focus on specific business scenarios where lock conflicts are most likely to occur, with analysis results driving targeted process improvements and system optimizations.

Next Steps

Begin with pilot implementation during controlled periods to understand your system's lock behavior patterns. Establish baseline metrics and gradually expand monitoring coverage to include all critical TM processes.

Consider integrating lock trace analysis into regular health checks and performance monitoring procedures. Document findings and resolutions to build organizational knowledge for future optimization efforts.

Conclusion

Effective lock trace analysis transforms TM performance management from reactive troubleshooting to proactive optimization. By leveraging TMLOCKTRACE02 and TMLOCKTRACE03, administrators gain the insights needed to maintain peak system performance and ensure smooth transportation operations.

The investment in understanding and implementing these tools pays dividends through improved user productivity, reduced system conflicts, and enhanced overall TM performance. Start your lock trace analysis journey today and unlock the full potential of your Transportation Management system.Refer:

3512098 - TM Lock Trace does not write the logs - SAP for Me

3050937 - Checkpoints in Transportation Management - SAP for Me

Hope that can help you.

Thanks, and regards

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Re: Unable view upload attachment icon in RAP - V4 UI
entry.content:
Also Please comment the annotation "@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true" in your CDS View. If this annotation is used then the Attachment will come as a text field and upload option will not appear.

Also check if BDEF is defined properly. The key field is not Read only.

Initially I also had this Icon, was not getting upload button. But when I defined the BDEF properly marking Key field as Read only on Update and commenting above annotation, it worked. I got the upload button.

Also, I have used ODATA V4 in Service Binding.

entry.title: Re: File Upload option not showing in SAP RAP Application
entry.content: Helps for me, thanks a lot. Worked after commenting the above annotation.

entry.title: Re: My experience with Joule for Consultants PoC
entry.content:
Hi @EgbertVenema , 

Good to see you here! 

J4C Has saved me a lot of time. Especially going through the conditions for printing in SD. I think this has saved me 2 days (spending 2 days instead of 4 days). Other questions saved me hours during a day. Overall, I think I am 25% more efficient. The fact that J4C mixes different solutions (Private, public, on/off prem)  is quite often not a problem: Processes are quite similar, and that's where I use it the most; quickly gain insight in the details of processes and questions about customizing.

Your question about saving time I would like to answer with an example: The last week I've used it to analyze performance problems in a distributed Fiori app. Especially being able to quickly rule out some common bottlenecks and having a plan for analysis gave me focus and efficiency. Other LLM's are less to the point as J4C (and I prefer not to use ChatGPT anymore since I learned that OpenAI's president is one of the biggest donors for Trumps movement and actively influencing legislation)

entry.title: Fresh Insights Every Two Months: What’s New in SAP for Utilities
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community,

The latest edition of our SAP Utilities Newsletter is now available! You may already have it in your inbox, if not, you can sign up here. 

 


Top Announcement: The New Utilities Customer Self-Service Agent

The newsletter leads with an exciting innovation: SAP’s new AI-powered customer self-service agent.
It delivers 24/7, human-like omnichannel support for common requests like:

  • meter readings
  • invoice questions
  • tariff changes
  • general account inquiries

It helps utilities reduce service costs while elevating customer satisfaction.
Readers are invited to the Espresso Session on February 19 for a live look.

Featured Highlights

SAP for Energy & Utilities Conference – Agenda Live

Our annual event will take place April 21–23, 2026, in Toulouse, and we’re excited to announce that the agenda is now live—along with the submission page for our new Energy & Utilities Innovation Awards. This year’s program covers the industry’s most important topics, including AI, customer experience, distributed energy resources, water management, circularity, and asset & service management—and much more. We look forward to welcoming you to France!

Innovative Billing Models

Dynamic tariffs and new billing scenarios are becoming essential.
A recent Espresso Session demonstrates how SAP supports these trends with live demos.

Smarter Emergency Response

A new SAP Field Service Management video shows intelligent automation for incident prioritization and resource dispatching.

Customer Success Stories

This bimonthly edition includes digital transformation stories from around the world:

  • Meralco (Philippines): Moving to SAP Cloud ERP Private for modernized, innovation-ready operations.
  • Sonnedix: Replacing 13 legacy systems with SAP Cloud ERP to increase efficiency and accelerate financial closing.
  • Gasunie: Transforming procurement with SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network.
  • Illwerke vkw: Fast, stable brownfield migration to SAP S/4HANA.
  • MVM: Standardizing procurement using SAP Ariba Strategic Suite.

Cloud ERP Updates

A new video showcases enhancements to Budget Billing Plans in SAP S/4HANA Utilities, now accessible directly within the Business Partner Financial Overview App across several billing procedures.

Customer Experience & AI Insights

  • A podcast with Fridrik Larsen and Kim Maren Ekrutt explores how AI enhances CX and where human support remains essential.
  • The next Espresso Session on March 25 highlights how SAP Emarsys for Utilities enables personalized, automated customer journeys.
Many more product updates, events, and company news are waiting for you.You can read the full newsletter here and don't forget to sign up.

entry.title: The Faces of Energy and Natural Resources at SAP
entry.content:
Energy and natural resources keep the world going.  The Energy and Natural Resources team at SAP is dedicated to fostering innovation and excellence across their industries. This team includes experts from the oil, gas & energy, utilities, chemicals, mill products, and mining industries.

By actively engaging with customers, partners, and stakeholders, and anticipating future trends, the team formulates strategies and solutions. Their collaborative efforts ensure they meet industry needs, stay at the forefront of advancements, drive positive change, and create value for our customers.

Recently, Torsten Welte took over the position as head of Energy and Natural Resources. Check out his announcement here: 

 

In the ongoing series Faces of ENR, Torsten talks to different team members every week, discovering their personal motivation and connection to the industry, current topics, and the value SAP delivers for each industry.

Mill Products and Mining

 

Utilities

 

 

 

 

Chemicals

Stay tuned for the first Chemicals video, appearing soon.

Oil, Gas, and Energy

Torsten's conversations with our oil, gas, and energy colleagues will be published soon. 

Stay connected to meet further team members and to learn more about the passionate team driving innovation for Utilities, Oil, Gas & Energy, Mill & Mining, and Chemicals.
